chapters.en: And I will establish my covenant with thee; and thou shalt know that I the LORD:

chapters.zh: 好使你在我赦免你一切所行的时候，心里追念，自觉抱愧，又因你的羞辱就不再开口。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: That thou mayest remember, and be confounded, and never open thy mouth any more because of thy shame, when I am pacified toward thee for all that thou hast done, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要向以色列家出谜语，设比喻，
chapters.en: Son of man, put forth a riddle, and speak a parable unto the house of Israel;

chapters.zh: 说主耶和华如此说：有一大鹰，翅膀大，翎毛长，羽毛丰满，彩色俱备，来到利巴嫩，将香柏树梢拧去，
chapters.en: And say, Thus saith the Lord GOD; A great eagle with great wings, longwinged, full of feathers, which had divers colours, came unto Lebanon, and took the highest branch of the cedar:

chapters.zh: 就是折去香柏树尽尖的嫩枝，叼到贸易之地，放在买卖城中；
chapters.en: He cropped off the top of his young twigs, and carried it into a land of traffick; he set it in a city of merchants.

chapters.zh: 又将以色列地的枝子栽于肥田里，插在大水旁，如插柳树，
chapters.en: He took also of the seed of the land, and planted it in a fruitful field; he placed by great waters, set it a willow tree.

chapters.zh: 就渐渐生长，成为蔓延矮小的葡萄树。其枝转向那鹰，其根在鹰以下，于是成了葡萄树，生出枝子，发出小枝。
chapters.en: And it grew, and became a spreading vine of low stature, whose branches turned toward him, and the roots thereof were under him: so it became a vine, and brought forth branches, and shot forth sprigs.

chapters.zh: 又有一大鹰，翅膀大，羽毛多。这葡萄树从栽种的畦中向这鹰弯过根来，发出枝子，好得他的浇灌。
chapters.en: There was also another great eagle with great wings and many feathers: and, behold, this vine did bend her roots toward him, and shot forth her branches toward him, that he might water it by the furrows of her plantation.

chapters.zh: 这树栽于肥田多水的旁边，好生枝子，结果子，成为佳美的葡萄树。
chapters.en: It was planted in a good soil by great waters, that it might bring forth branches, and that it might bear fruit, that it might be a goodly vine.

chapters.zh: 你要说，主耶和华如此说：这葡萄树岂能发旺呢？鹰岂不拔出他的根来，芟除他的果子，使他枯干，使他发的嫩叶都枯干了么？也不用大力和多民，就拔出他的根来。
chapters.en: Say thou, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Shall it prosper? shall he not pull up the roots thereof, and cut off the fruit thereof, that it wither? it shall wither in all the leaves of her spring, even without great power or many people to pluck it up by the roots thereof.

chapters.zh: 葡萄树虽然栽种，岂能发旺呢？一经东风，岂不全然枯干么？必在生长的畦中枯干了。
chapters.en: Yea, behold, planted, shall it prosper? shall it not utterly wither, when the east wind toucheth it? it shall wither in the furrows where it grew.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Moreover the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 你对那悖逆之家说：你们不知道这些事是甚么意思么？你要告诉他们说，巴比伦王曾到耶路撒冷，将其中的君王和首领带到巴比伦自己那里去。
chapters.en: Say now to the rebellious house, Know ye not what these ? tell , Behold, the king of Babylon is come to Jerusalem, and hath taken the king thereof, and the princes thereof, and led them with him to Babylon;

chapters.zh: 从以色列的宗室中取一人与他立约，使他发誓，并将国中有势力的人掳去，
chapters.en: And hath taken of the king's seed, and made a covenant with him, and hath taken an oath of him: he hath also taken the mighty of the land:

chapters.zh: 使国低微不能自强，惟因守盟约得以存立。
chapters.en: That the kingdom might be base, that it might not lift itself up, that by keeping of his covenant it might stand.

chapters.zh: 他却背叛巴比伦王，打发使者往埃及去，要他们给他马匹和多民。他岂能亨通呢？行这样事的人岂能逃脱呢？他背约岂能逃脱呢？
chapters.en: But he rebelled against him in sending his ambassadors into Egypt, that they might give him horses and much people. Shall he prosper? shall he escape that doeth such ? or shall he break the covenant, and be delivered?

chapters.zh: 他轻看向王所起的誓，背弃王与他所立的约。主耶和华说：我指著我的永生起誓，他定要死在立他作王、巴比伦王的京都。
chapters.en: I live, saith the Lord GOD, surely in the place the king that made him king, whose oath he despised, and whose covenant he brake, with him in the midst of Babylon he shall die.

chapters.zh: 敌人筑垒造台，与他打仗的时候，为要剪除多人，法老虽领大军队和大群众，还是不能帮助他。
chapters.en: Neither shall Pharaoh with mighty army and great company make for him in the war, by casting up mounts, and building forts, to cut off many persons:

chapters.zh: 他轻看誓言，背弃盟约，已经投降，却又做这一切的事，他必不能逃脱。
chapters.en: Seeing he despised the oath by breaking the covenant, when, lo, he had given his hand, and hath done all these , he shall not escape.

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：我指著我的永生起誓，他既轻看指我所起的誓，背弃指我所立的约，我必要使这罪归在他头上。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; I live, surely mine oath that he hath despised, and my covenant that he hath broken, even it will I recompense upon his own head.

chapters.zh: 我必将我的网撒在他身上，他必在我的网罗中缠住。我必带他到巴比伦，并要在那里因他干犯我的罪刑罚他。
chapters.en: And I will spread my net upon him, and he shall be taken in my snare, and I will bring him to Babylon, and will plead with him there for his trespass that he hath trespassed against me.

chapters.zh: 他的一切军队，凡逃跑的，都必倒在刀下；所剩下的，也必分散四方（方：原文是风）。你们就知道说这话的是我 ─ 耶和华。
chapters.en: And all his fugitives with all his bands shall fall by the sword, and they that remain shall be scattered toward all winds: and ye shall know that I the LORD have spoken .

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我要将香柏树梢拧去栽上，就是从尽尖的嫩枝中折一嫩枝，栽于极高的山上；
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; I will also take of the highest branch of the high cedar, and will set ; I will crop off from the top of his young twigs a tender one, and will plant upon an high mountain and eminent:

chapters.zh: 在以色列高处的山栽上。他就生枝子，结果子，成为佳美的香柏树，各类飞鸟都必宿在其下，就是宿在枝子的荫下。
chapters.en: In the mountain of the height of Israel will I plant it: and it shall bring forth boughs, and bear fruit, and be a goodly cedar: and under it shall dwell all fowl of every wing; in the shadow of the branches thereof shall they dwell.

chapters.zh: 田野的树木都必知道我 ─ 耶和华使高树矮小，矮树高大；青树枯干，枯树发旺。我 ─ 耶和华如此说，也如此行了。
chapters.en: And all the trees of the field shall know that I the LORD have brought down the high tree, have exalted the low tree, have dried up the green tree, and have made the dry tree to flourish: I the LORD have spoken and have done .

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: The word of the LORD came unto me again, saying,

chapters.zh: 你们在以色列地怎么用这俗语说父亲吃了酸葡萄，儿子的牙酸倒了呢？
chapters.en: What mean ye, that ye use this proverb concerning the land of Israel, saying, The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge?

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：我指著我的永生起誓，你们在以色列中，必不再有用这俗语的因由。
chapters.en: I live, saith the Lord GOD, ye shall not have any more to use this proverb in Israel.

chapters.zh: 看哪，世人都是属我的；为父的怎样属我，为子的也照样属我；犯罪的，他必死亡。
chapters.en: Behold, all souls are mine; as the soul of the father, so also the soul of the son is mine: the soul that sinneth, it shall die.

chapters.zh: 人若是公义，且行正直与合理的事：
chapters.en: But if a man be just, and do that which is lawful and right,

chapters.zh: 未曾在山上吃过祭偶像之物，未曾仰望以色列家的偶像，未曾玷污邻舍的妻，未曾在妇人的经期内亲近他，
chapters.en: hath not eaten upon the mountains, neither hath lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, neither hath defiled his neighbour's wife, neither hath come near to a menstruous woman,

chapters.zh: 未曾亏负人，乃将欠债之人的当头还给他；未曾抢夺人的物件，却将食物给饥饿的人吃，将衣服给赤身的人穿；
chapters.en: And hath not oppressed any, hath restored to the debtor his pledge, hath spoiled none by violence, hath given his bread to the hungry, and hath covered the naked with a garment;

chapters.zh: 未曾向借钱的弟兄取利，也未曾向借粮的弟兄多要，缩手不作罪孽，在两人之间，按至理判断；
chapters.en: He hath not given forth upon usury, neither hath taken any increase, hath withdrawn his hand from iniquity, hath executed true judgment between man and man,

chapters.zh: 遵行我的律例，谨守我的典章，按诚实行事 ─ 这人是公义的，必定存活。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Hath walked in my statutes, and hath kept my judgments, to deal truly; he just, he shall surely live, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 他若生一个儿子，作强盗，是流人血的，不行以上所说之善，反行其中之恶，乃在山上吃过祭偶像之物，并玷污邻舍的妻，
chapters.en: If he beget a son a robber, a shedder of blood, and doeth the like to one of these ,

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: And that doeth not any of those , but even hath eaten upon the mountains, and defiled his neighbour's wife,

chapters.zh: 亏负困苦和穷乏的人，抢夺人的物，未曾将当头还给人，仰望偶像，并行可憎的事，
chapters.en: Hath oppressed the poor and needy, hath spoiled by violence, hath not restored the pledge, and hath lifted up his eyes to the idols, hath committed abomination,

chapters.zh: 向借钱的弟兄取利，向借粮的弟兄多要 ─ 这人岂能存活呢？他必不能存活。他行这一切可憎的事，必要死亡，他的罪（原文是血）必归到他身上。
chapters.en: Hath given forth upon usury, and hath taken increase: shall he then live? he shall not live: he hath done all these abominations; he shall surely die; his blood shall be upon him.

chapters.zh: 他若生一个儿子，见父亲所犯的一切罪便惧怕（有古卷：思量），不照样去做；
chapters.en: Now, lo, he beget a son, that seeth all his father's sins which he hath done, and considereth, and doeth not such like,

chapters.zh: 未曾在山上吃过祭偶像之物，未曾仰望以色列家的偶像，未曾玷污邻舍的妻，
chapters.en: hath not eaten upon the mountains, neither hath lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, hath not defiled his neighbour's wife,

chapters.zh: 未曾亏负人，未曾取人的当头，未曾抢夺人的物件，却将食物给饥饿的人吃，将衣服给赤身的人穿，
chapters.en: Neither hath oppressed any, hath not withholden the pledge, neither hath spoiled by violence, hath given his bread to the hungry, and hath covered the naked with a garment,

chapters.zh: 缩手不害贫穷人，未曾向借钱的弟兄取利，也未曾向借粮的弟兄多要；他顺从我的典章，遵行我的律例，就不因父亲的罪孽死亡，定要存活。
chapters.en: hath taken off his hand from the poor, hath not received usury nor increase, hath executed my judgments, hath walked in my statutes; he shall not die for the iniquity of his father, he shall surely live.

chapters.zh: 至于他父亲；因为欺人太甚，抢夺弟兄，在本国的民中行不善，他必因自己的罪孽死亡。
chapters.en: his father, because he cruelly oppressed, spoiled his brother by violence, and did which not good among his people, lo, even he shall die in his iniquity.

chapters.zh: 你们还说：儿子为何不担当父亲的罪孽呢？儿子行正直与合理的事，谨守遵行我的一切律例，他必定存活。
chapters.en: Yet say ye, Why? doth not the son bear the iniquity of the father? When the son hath done that which is lawful and right, hath kept all my statutes, and hath done them, he shall surely live.

chapters.zh: 惟有犯罪的，他必死亡。儿子必不担当父亲的罪孽，父亲也不担当儿子的罪孽。义人的善果必归自己，恶人的恶报也必归自己。
chapters.en: The soul that sinneth, it shall die. The son shall not bear the iniquity of the father, neither shall the father bear the iniquity of the son: the righteousness of the righteous shall be upon him, and the wickedness of the wicked shall be upon him.

chapters.zh: 恶人若回头离开所做的一切罪恶，谨守我一切的律例，行正直与合理的事，他必定存活，不致死亡。
chapters.en: But if the wicked will turn from all his sins that he hath committed, and keep all my statutes, and do that which is lawful and right, he shall surely live, he shall not die.

chapters.zh: 他所犯的一切罪过都不被记念，因所行的义，他必存活。
chapters.en: All his transgressions that he hath committed, they shall not be mentioned unto him: in his righteousness that he hath done he shall live.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：恶人死亡，岂是我喜悦的么？不是喜悦他回头离开所行的道存活么？
chapters.en: Have I any pleasure at all that the wicked should die? saith the Lord GOD: not that he should return from his ways, and live?

chapters.zh: 义人若转离义行而作罪孽，照著恶人所行一切可憎的事而行，他岂能存活么？他所行的一切义都不被记念；他必因所犯的罪、所行的恶死亡。
chapters.en: But when the righteous turneth away from his righteousness, and committeth iniquity, doeth according to all the abominations that the wicked doeth, shall he live? All his righteousness that he hath done shall not be mentioned: in his trespass that he hath trespassed, and in his sin that he hath sinned, in them shall he die.

chapters.zh: 你们还说：主的道不公平！以色列家啊，你们当听，我的道岂不公平么？你们的道岂不是不公平么？
chapters.en: Yet ye say, The way of the Lord is not equal. Hear now, O house of Israel; Is not my way equal? are not your ways unequal?

chapters.zh: 义人若转离义行而作罪孽死亡，他是因所作的罪孽死亡。
chapters.en: When a righteous turneth away from his righteousness, and committeth iniquity, and dieth in them; for his iniquity that he hath done shall he die.

chapters.zh: 再者，恶人若回头离开所行的恶，行正直与合理的事，他必将性命救活了。
chapters.en: Again, when the wicked turneth away from his wickedness that he hath committed, and doeth that which is lawful and right, he shall save his soul alive.

chapters.zh: 因为他思量，回头离开所犯的一切罪过，必定存活，不致死亡。
chapters.en: Because he considereth, and turneth away from all his transgressions that he hath committed, he shall surely live, he shall not die.

chapters.zh: 以色列家还说：主的道不公平！以色列家啊，我的道岂不公平么？你们的道岂不是不公平么？
chapters.en: Yet saith the house of Israel, The way of the Lord is not equal. O house of Israel, are not my ways equal? are not your ways unequal?

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华说：以色列家啊，我必按你们各人所行的审判你们。你们当回头离开所犯的一切罪过。这样，罪孽必不使你们败亡。
chapters.en: Therefore I will judge you, O house of Israel, every one according to his ways, saith the Lord GOD. Repent, and turn from all your transgressions; so iniquity shall not be your ruin.

chapters.zh: 你们要将所犯的一切罪过尽行抛弃，自做一个新心和新灵。以色列家啊，你们何必死亡呢？
chapters.en: Cast away from you all your transgressions, whereby ye have transgressed; and make you a new heart and a new spirit: for why will ye die, O house of Israel?

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：我不喜悦那死人之死，所以你们当回头而存活。
chapters.en: For I have no pleasure in the death of him that dieth, saith the Lord GOD: wherefore turn , and live ye.

chapters.zh: 你当为以色列的王作起哀歌，
chapters.en: Moreover take thou up a lamentation for the princes of Israel,

chapters.zh: 说：你的母亲是甚么呢？是个母狮子，蹲伏在狮子中间，在少壮狮子中养育小狮子。
chapters.en: And say, What thy mother? A lioness: she lay down among lions, she nourished her whelps among young lions.

chapters.zh: 在他小狮子中养大一个，成了少壮狮子，学会抓食而吃人。
chapters.en: And she brought up one of her whelps: it became a young lion, and it learned to catch the prey; it devoured men.

chapters.zh: 列国听见了就把他捉在他们的坑中，用钩子拉到埃及地去。
chapters.en: The nations also heard of him; he was taken in their pit, and they brought him with chains unto the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 母狮见自己等候失了指望，就从他小狮子中又将一个养为少壮狮子。
chapters.en: Now when she saw that she had waited, her hope was lost, then she took another of her whelps, made him a young lion.

chapters.zh: 他在众狮子中走来走去，成了少壮狮子，学会抓食而吃人。
chapters.en: And he went up and down among the lions, he became a young lion, and learned to catch the prey, devoured men.

chapters.zh: 他知道列国的宫殿，又使他们的城邑变为荒场；因他咆哮的声音，遍地和其中所有的就都荒废。
chapters.en: And he knew their desolate palaces, and he laid waste their cities; and the land was desolate, and the fulness thereof, by the noise of his roaring.

chapters.zh: 于是四围邦国各省的人来攻击他，将网撒在他身上，捉在他们的坑中。
chapters.en: Then the nations set against him on every side from the provinces, and spread their net over him: he was taken in their pit.

chapters.zh: 他们用钩子钩住他，将他放在笼中，带到巴比伦王那里，将他放入坚固之所，使他的声音在以色列山上不再听见。
chapters.en: And they put him in ward in chains, and brought him to the king of Babylon: they brought him into holds, that his voice should no more be heard upon the mountains of Israel.

chapters.zh: 你的母亲先前如葡萄树，极其茂盛（原文是在你血中），栽于水旁。因为水多，就多结果子，满生枝子；
chapters.en: Thy mother like a vine in thy blood, planted by the waters: she was fruitful and full of branches by reason of many waters.

chapters.zh: 生出坚固的枝干，可作掌权者的杖。这枝干高举在茂密的枝中，而且他生长高大，枝子繁多，远远可见。
chapters.en: And she had strong rods for the sceptres of them that bare rule, and her stature was exalted among the thick branches, and she appeared in her height with the multitude of her branches.

chapters.zh: 但这葡萄树因忿怒被拔出摔在地上；东风吹干其上的果子，坚固的枝干折断枯干，被火烧毁了；
chapters.en: But she was plucked up in fury, she was cast down to the ground, and the east wind dried up her fruit: her strong rods were broken and withered; the fire consumed them.

chapters.zh: 如今栽于旷野干旱无水之地。
chapters.en: And now she planted in the wilderness, in a dry and thirsty ground.

chapters.zh: 火也从他枝干中发出，烧灭果子，以致没有坚固的枝干可作掌权者的杖。这是哀歌，也必用以作哀歌。
chapters.en: And fire is gone out of a rod of her branches, hath devoured her fruit, so that she hath no strong rod a sceptre to rule. This a lamentation, and shall be for a lamentation.

chapters.zh: 第七年五月初十日，有以色列的几个长老来求问耶和华，坐在我面前。
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the seventh year, in the fifth , the tenth of the month, certain of the elders of Israel came to enquire of the LORD, and sat before me.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Then came the word of the LORD unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要告诉以色列的长老说，主耶和华如此说：你们来是求问我么？主耶和华说：我指著我的永生起誓，我必不被你们求问。
chapters.en: Son of man, speak unto the elders of Israel, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Are ye come to enquire of me? I live, saith the Lord GOD, I will not be enquired of by you.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要审问审问他们么？你当使他们知道他们列祖那些可憎的事，
chapters.en: Wilt thou judge them, son of man, wilt thou judge ? cause them to know the abominations of their fathers:

chapters.zh: 对他们说，主耶和华如此说：当日我拣选以色列，向雅各家的后裔起誓，在埃及地将自己向他们显现，说：我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: And say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; In the day when I chose Israel, and lifted up mine hand unto the seed of the house of Jacob, and made myself known unto them in the land of Egypt, when I lifted up mine hand unto them, saying, I the LORD your God;

chapters.zh: 那日我向他们起誓，必领他们出埃及地，到我为他们察看的流奶与蜜之地；那地在万国中是有荣耀的。
chapters.en: In the day I lifted up mine hand unto them, to bring them forth of the land of Egypt into a land that I had espied for them, flowing with milk and honey, which the glory of all lands:

chapters.zh: 我对他们说，你们各人要抛弃眼所喜爱那可憎之物，不可因埃及的偶像玷污自己。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: Then said I unto them, Cast ye away every man the abominations of his eyes, and defile not yourselves with the idols of Egypt: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 他们却悖逆我，不肯听从我，不抛弃他们眼所喜爱那可憎之物，不离弃埃及的偶像。我就说，我要将我的忿怒倾在他们身上，在埃及地向他们成就我怒中所定的。
chapters.en: But they rebelled against me, and would not hearken unto me: they did not every man cast away the abominations of their eyes, neither did they forsake the idols of Egypt: then I said, I will pour out my fury upon them, to accomplish my anger against them in the midst of the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 我却为我名的缘故没有这样行，免得我名在他们所住的列国人眼前被亵渎；我领他们出埃及地，在这列国人的眼前将自己向他们显现。
chapters.en: But I wrought for my name's sake, that it should not be polluted before the heathen, among whom they , in whose sight I made myself known unto them, in bringing them forth out of the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 这样，我就使他们出埃及地，领他们到旷野，
chapters.en: Wherefore I caused them to go forth out of the land of Egypt, and brought them into the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 将我的律例赐给他们，将我的典章指示他们；人若遵行就必因此活著。
chapters.en: And I gave them my statutes, and shewed them my judgments, which a man do, he shall even live in them.

chapters.zh: 又将我的安息日赐给他们，好在我与他们中间为证据，使他们知道我 ─ 耶和华是叫他们成为圣的。
chapters.en: Moreover also I gave them my sabbaths, to be a sign between me and them, that they might know that I the LORD that sanctify them.

chapters.zh: 以色列家却在旷野悖逆我，不顺从我的律例，厌弃我的典章（人若遵行就必因此活著），大大干犯我的安息日。我就说，要在旷野将我的忿怒倾在他们身上，灭绝他们。
chapters.en: But the house of Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness: they walked not in my statutes, and they despised my judgments, which a man do, he shall even live in them; and my sabbaths they greatly polluted: then I said, I would pour out my fury upon them in the wilderness, to consume them.

chapters.zh: 我却为我名的缘故，没有这样行，免得我的名在我领他们出埃及的列国人眼前被亵渎。
chapters.en: But I wrought for my name's sake, that it should not be polluted before the heathen, in whose sight I brought them out.

chapters.zh: 并且我在旷野向他们起誓，必不领他们进入我所赐给他们流奶与蜜之地（那地在万国中是有荣耀的）；
chapters.en: Yet also I lifted up my hand unto them in the wilderness, that I would not bring them into the land which I had given , flowing with milk and honey, which the glory of all lands;

chapters.zh: 因为他们厌弃我的典章，不顺从我的律例，干犯我的安息日，他们的心随从自己的偶像。
chapters.en: Because they despised my judgments, and walked not in my statutes, but polluted my sabbaths: for their heart went after their idols.

chapters.zh: 虽然如此，我眼仍顾惜他们，不毁灭他们，不在旷野将他们灭绝净尽。
chapters.en: Nevertheless mine eye spared them from destroying them, neither did I make an end of them in the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 我在旷野对他们的儿女说：不要遵行你们父亲的律例，不要谨守他们的恶规，也不要因他们的偶像玷污自己。
chapters.en: But I said unto their children in the wilderness, Walk ye not in the statutes of your fathers, neither observe their judgments, nor defile yourselves with their idols:

chapters.zh: 我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神，你们要顺从我的律例，谨守遵行我的典章，
chapters.en: I the LORD your God; walk in my statutes, and keep my judgments, and do them;

chapters.zh: 且以我的安息日为圣。这日在我与你们中间为证据，使你们知道我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: And hallow my sabbaths; and they shall be a sign between me and you, that ye may know that I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 只是他们的儿女悖逆我，不顺从我的律例，也不谨守遵行我的典章（人若遵行就必因此活著），干犯我的安息日。我就说，要将我的忿怒倾在他们身上，在旷野向他们成就我怒中所定的。
chapters.en: Notwithstanding the children rebelled against me: they walked not in my statutes, neither kept my judgments to do them, which a man do, he shall even live in them; they polluted my sabbaths: then I said, I would pour out my fury upon them, to accomplish my anger against them in the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 虽然如此，我却为我名的缘故缩手没有这样行，免得我的名在我领他们出埃及的列国人眼前被亵渎。
chapters.en: Nevertheless I withdrew mine hand, and wrought for my name's sake, that it should not be polluted in the sight of the heathen, in whose sight I brought them forth.

chapters.zh: 并且我在旷野向他们起誓，必将他们分散在列国，四散在列邦；
chapters.en: I lifted up mine hand unto them also in the wilderness, that I would scatter them among the heathen, and disperse them through the countries;

chapters.zh: 因为他们不遵行我的典章，竟厌弃我的律例，干犯我的安息日，眼目仰望他们父亲的偶像。
chapters.en: Because they had not executed my judgments, but had despised my statutes, and had polluted my sabbaths, and their eyes were after their fathers' idols.

chapters.zh: 我也任他们遵行不美的律例，谨守不能使人活著的恶规。
chapters.en: Wherefore I gave them also statutes not good, and judgments whereby they should not live;

chapters.zh: 因他们将一切头生的经火，我就任凭他们在这供献的事上玷污自己，好叫他们凄凉，使他们知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And I polluted them in their own gifts, in that they caused to pass through all that openeth the womb, that I might make them desolate, to the end that they might know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要告诉以色列家说，主耶和华如此说：你们的列祖在得罪我的事上亵渎我；
chapters.en: Therefore, son of man, speak unto the house of Israel, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Yet in this your fathers have blasphemed me, in that they have committed a trespass against me.

chapters.zh: 因为我领他们到了我起誓应许赐给他们的地，他们看见各高山、各茂密树，就在那里献祭，奉上惹我发怒的供物，也在那里焚烧馨香的祭牲，并浇上奠祭。
chapters.en: when I had brought them into the land, the which I lifted up mine hand to give it to them, then they saw every high hill, and all the thick trees, and they offered there their sacrifices, and there they presented the provocation of their offering: there also they made their sweet savour, and poured out there their drink offerings.

chapters.zh: 我就对他们说：你们所上的那高处叫甚么呢？（那高处的名字叫巴麻直到今日。）
chapters.en: Then I said unto them, What the high place whereunto ye go? And the name thereof is called Bamah unto this day.

chapters.zh: 所以你要对以色列家说，主耶和华如此说：你们仍照你们列祖所行的玷污自己么？仍照他们可憎的事行邪淫么？
chapters.en: Wherefore say unto the house of Israel, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Are ye polluted after the manner of your fathers? and commit ye whoredom after their abominations?

chapters.zh: 你们奉上供物使你们儿子经火的时候，仍将一切偶像玷污自己，直到今日么？以色列家啊，我岂被你们求问么？主耶和华说：我指著我的永生起誓，我必不被你们求问。
chapters.en: For when ye offer your gifts, when ye make your sons to pass through the fire, ye pollute yourselves with all your idols, even unto this day: and shall I be enquired of by you, O house of Israel? I live, saith the Lord GOD, I will not be enquired of by you.

chapters.zh: 你们说：我们要像外邦人和列国的宗族一样，去事奉木头与石头。你们所起的这心意万不能成就。
chapters.en: And that which cometh into your mind shall not be at all, that ye say, We will be as the heathen, as the families of the countries, to serve wood and stone.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：我指著我的永生起誓，我总要作王，用大能的手和伸出来的膀臂，并倾出来的忿怒，治理你们。
chapters.en: I live, saith the Lord GOD, surely with a mighty hand, and with a stretched out arm, and with fury poured out, will I rule over you:

chapters.zh: 我必用大能的手和伸出来的膀臂，并倾出来的忿怒，将你们从万民中领出来，从分散的列国内聚集你们。
chapters.en: And I will bring you out from the people, and will gather you out of the countries wherein ye are scattered, with a mighty hand, and with a stretched out arm, and with fury poured out.

chapters.zh: 我必带你们到外邦人的旷野，在那里当面刑罚你们。
chapters.en: And I will bring you into the wilderness of the people, and there will I plead with you face to face.

chapters.zh: 我怎样在埃及地的旷野刑罚你们的列祖，也必照样刑罚你们。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Like as I pleaded with your fathers in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so will I plead with you, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 我必使你们从杖下经过，使你们被约拘束。
chapters.en: And I will cause you to pass under the rod, and I will bring you into the bond of the covenant:

chapters.zh: 我必从你们中间除净叛逆和得罪我的人，将他们从所寄居的地方领出来，他们却不得入以色列地。你们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And I will purge out from among you the rebels, and them that transgress against me: I will bring them forth out of the country where they sojourn, and they shall not enter into the land of Israel: and ye shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 以色列家啊，至于你们，主耶和华如此说：从此以后若不听从我，就任凭你们去事奉偶像，只是不可再因你们的供物和偶像亵渎我的圣名。
chapters.en: As for you, O house of Israel, thus saith the Lord GOD; Go ye, serve ye every one his idols, and hereafter , if ye will not hearken unto me: but pollute ye my holy name no more with your gifts, and with your idols.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：在我的圣山，就是以色列高处的山，所有以色列的全家都要事奉我。我要在那里悦纳你们，向你们要供物和初熟的土产，并一切的圣物。
chapters.en: For in mine holy mountain, in the mountain of the height of Israel, saith the Lord GOD, there shall all the house of Israel, all of them in the land, serve me: there will I accept them, and there will I require your offerings, and the firstfruits of your oblations, with all your holy things.

chapters.zh: 我从万民中领你们出来，从分散的列国内聚集你们，那时我必悦纳你们好像馨香之祭，要在外邦人眼前在你们身上显为圣。
chapters.en: I will accept you with your sweet savour, when I bring you out from the people, and gather you out of the countries wherein ye have been scattered; and I will be sanctified in you before the heathen.

chapters.zh: 我领你们进入以色列地，就是我起誓应许赐给你们列祖之地，那时你们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And ye shall know that I the LORD, when I shall bring you into the land of Israel, into the country the which I lifted up mine hand to give it to your fathers.

chapters.zh: 你们在那里要追念玷污自己的行动作为，又要因所做的一切恶事厌恶自己。
chapters.en: And there shall ye remember your ways, and all your doings, wherein ye have been defiled; and ye shall lothe yourselves in your own sight for all your evils that ye have committed.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：以色列家啊，我为我名的缘故，不照著你们的恶行和你们的坏事待你们；你们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And ye shall know that I the LORD, when I have wrought with you for my name's sake, not according to your wicked ways, nor according to your corrupt doings, O ye house of Israel, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Moreover the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要面向南方，向南滴下预言攻击南方田野的树林。
chapters.en: Son of man, set thy face toward the south, and drop toward the south, and prophesy against the forest of the south field;

chapters.zh: 对南方的树林说，要听耶和华的话。主耶和华如此说：我必使火在你中间著起，烧灭你中间的一切青树和枯树，猛烈的火燄必不熄灭。从南到北，人的脸面都被烧焦。
chapters.en: And say to the forest of the south, Hear the word of the LORD; Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will kindle a fire in thee, and it shall devour every green tree in thee, and every dry tree: the flaming flame shall not be quenched, and all faces from the south to the north shall be burned therein.

chapters.zh: 凡有血气的都必知道是我 ─ 耶和华使火著起，这火必不熄灭。
chapters.en: And all flesh shall see that I the LORD have kindled it: it shall not be quenched.

chapters.zh: 于是我说：哎！主耶和华啊，人都指著我说：他岂不是说比喻的么？
chapters.en: Then said I, Ah Lord GOD! they say of me, Doth he not speak parables?

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要面向耶路撒冷和圣所滴下预言，攻击以色列地。
chapters.en: Son of man, set thy face toward Jerusalem, and drop toward the holy places, and prophesy against the land of Israel,

chapters.zh: 对以色列地说，耶和华如此说：我与你为敌，并要拔刀出鞘，从你中间将义人和恶人一并剪除。
chapters.en: And say to the land of Israel, Thus saith the LORD; Behold, I against thee, and will draw forth my sword out of his sheath, and will cut off from thee the righteous and the wicked.

chapters.zh: 我既要从你中间剪除义人和恶人，所以我的刀要出鞘，自南至北攻击一切有血气的；
chapters.en: Seeing then that I will cut off from thee the righteous and the wicked, therefore shall my sword go forth out of his sheath against all flesh from the south to the north:

chapters.zh: 一切有血气的就知道我 ─ 耶和华已经拔刀出鞘，必不再入鞘。
chapters.en: That all flesh may know that I the LORD have drawn forth my sword out of his sheath: it shall not return any more.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要叹息，在他们眼前弯著腰，苦苦地叹息。
chapters.en: Sigh therefore, thou son of man, with the breaking of loins; and with bitterness sigh before their eyes.

chapters.zh: 他们问你说：为何叹息呢 ﹖ 你就说：因为有风声、灾祸要来。人心都必消化，手都发软，精神衰败，膝弱如水。看哪，这灾祸临近，必然成就。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And it shall be, when they say unto thee, Wherefore sighest thou? that thou shalt answer, For the tidings; because it cometh: and every heart shall melt, and all hands shall be feeble, and every spirit shall faint, and all knees shall be weak water: behold, it cometh, and shall be brought to pass, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Again the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要预言。耶和华吩咐我如此说：有刀、有刀，是磨快擦亮的；
chapters.en: Son of man, prophesy, and say, Thus saith the LORD; Say, A sword, a sword is sharpened, and also furbished:

chapters.zh: 磨快为要行杀戮，擦亮为要像闪电。我们岂可快乐么 ﹖ 罚我子的杖藐视各树。
chapters.en: It is sharpened to make a sore slaughter; it is furbished that it may glitter: should we then make mirth? it contemneth the rod of my son, every tree.

chapters.zh: 这刀已经交给人擦亮，为要应手使用。这刀已经磨快擦亮，好交在行杀戮的人手中。
chapters.en: And he hath given it to be furbished, that it may be handled: this sword is sharpened, and it is furbished, to give it into the hand of the slayer.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要呼喊哀号，因为这刀临到我的百姓和以色列一切的首领。他们和我的百姓都交在刀下，所以你要拍腿叹息。
chapters.en: Cry and howl, son of man: for it shall be upon my people, it upon all the princes of Israel: terrors by reason of the sword shall be upon my people: smite therefore upon thigh.

chapters.zh: 有试验的事；若那藐视的杖归于无有，怎么样呢 ﹖ 这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Because a trial, and what if contemn even the rod? it shall be no , saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要拍掌预言。我 ─ 耶和华要使这刀，就是致死伤的刀，一连三次加倍刺人，进入他们的内屋，使大人受死伤的就是这刀。
chapters.en: Thou therefore, son of man, prophesy, and smite hands together, and let the sword be doubled the third time, the sword of the slain: it the sword of the great slain, which entereth into their privy chambers.

chapters.zh: 我设立这恐吓人的刀，攻击他们的一切城门，使他们的心消化，加增他们跌倒的事。哎！这刀造得像闪电，磨得尖利，要行杀戮。
chapters.en: I have set the point of the sword against all their gates, that heart may faint, and ruins be multiplied: ah! made bright, wrapped up for the slaughter.

chapters.zh: 刀啊，你归在右边，摆在左边；你面向哪方，就向那方杀戮。
chapters.en: Go thee one way or other, on the right hand, on the left, whithersoever thy face set.

chapters.zh: 我也要拍掌，并要使我的忿怒止息。这是我 ─ 耶和华说的。
chapters.en: I will also smite mine hands together, and I will cause my fury to rest: I the LORD have said .

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: The word of the LORD came unto me again, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要定出两条路，好使巴比伦王的刀来。这两条路必从一地分出来，又要在通城的路口上画出一只手来。
chapters.en: Also, thou son of man, appoint thee two ways, that the sword of the king of Babylon may come: both twain shall come forth out of one land: and choose thou a place, choose at the head of the way to the city.

chapters.zh: 你要定出一条路，使刀来到亚扪人的拉巴；又要定出一条路，使刀来到犹大的坚固城耶路撒冷。
chapters.en: Appoint a way, that the sword may come to Rabbath of the Ammonites, and to Judah in Jerusalem the defenced.

chapters.zh: 因为巴比伦王站在岔路那里，在两条路口上要占卜。他摇签（原文是箭）求问神像，察看牺牲的肝；
chapters.en: For the king of Babylon stood at the parting of the way, at the head of the two ways, to use divination: he made arrows bright, he consulted with images, he looked in the liver.

chapters.zh: 在右手中拿著为耶路撒冷占卜的签，使他安设撞城锤，张口叫杀，扬声呐喊，筑垒造台，以撞城锤，攻打城门。
chapters.en: At his right hand was the divination for Jerusalem, to appoint captains, to open the mouth in the slaughter, to lift up the voice with shouting, to appoint rams against the gates, to cast a mount, to build a fort.

chapters.zh: 据那些曾起誓的犹大人看来，这是虚假的占卜；但巴比伦王要使他们想起罪孽，以致将他们捉住。
chapters.en: And it shall be unto them as a false divination in their sight, to them that have sworn oaths: but he will call to remembrance the iniquity, that they may be taken.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：因你们的过犯显露，使你们的罪孽被记念，以致你们的罪恶在行为上都彰显出来；又因你们被记念，就被捉住。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Because ye have made your iniquity to be remembered, in that your transgressions are discovered, so that in all your doings your sins do appear; because, , that ye are come to remembrance, ye shall be taken with the hand.

chapters.zh: 你这受死伤行恶的以色列王啊，罪孽的尽头到了，受报的日子已到。
chapters.en: And thou, profane wicked prince of Israel, whose day is come, when iniquity an end,

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：当除掉冠，摘下冕，景况必不再像先前；要使卑者升为高，使高者降为卑。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; Remove the diadem, and take off the crown: this not the same: exalt low, and abase high.

chapters.zh: 我要将这国倾覆，倾覆，而又倾覆；这国也必不再有，直等到那应得的人来到，我就赐给他。
chapters.en: I will overturn, overturn, overturn, it: and it shall be no , until he come whose right it is; and I will give it .

chapters.zh: 人子啊，要发预言说：主耶和华论到亚扪人和他们的凌辱，吩咐我如此说：有刀，有拔出来的刀，已经擦亮，为行杀戮，使他像闪电以行吞灭。
chapters.en: And thou, son of man, prophesy and say, Thus saith the Lord GOD concerning the Ammonites, and concerning their reproach; even say thou, The sword, the sword drawn: for the slaughter furbished, to consume because of the glittering:

chapters.zh: 人为你见虚假的异象，行谎诈的占卜，使你倒在受死伤之恶人的颈项上。他们罪孽到了尽头，受报的日子已到。
chapters.en: Whiles they see vanity unto thee, whiles they divine a lie unto thee, to bring thee upon the necks of slain, of the wicked, whose day is come, when their iniquity an end.

chapters.zh: 你将刀收入鞘罢！在你受造之处、生长之地，我必刑罚你。
chapters.en: Shall I cause to return into his sheath? I will judge thee in the place where thou wast created, in the land of thy nativity.

chapters.zh: 我必将我的恼恨倒在你身上，将我烈怒的火喷在你身上；又将你交在善于杀灭的畜类人手中。
chapters.en: And I will pour out mine indignation upon thee, I will blow against thee in the fire of my wrath, and deliver thee into the hand of brutish men, skilful to destroy.

chapters.zh: 你必当柴被火焚烧，你的血必流在国中，你必不再被记念，因为这是我 ─ 耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Thou shalt be for fuel to the fire; thy blood shall be in the midst of the land; thou shalt be no remembered: for I the LORD have spoken .

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: Moreover the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要审问审问这流人血的城么 ﹖ 当使他知道他一切可憎的事。
chapters.en: Now, thou son of man, wilt thou judge, wilt thou judge the bloody city? yea, thou shalt shew her all her abominations.

chapters.zh: 你要说，主耶和华如此说：哎！这城有流人血的事在其中，叫他受报的日期来到，又做偶像玷污自己，陷害自己。
chapters.en: Then say thou, Thus saith the Lord GOD, The city sheddeth blood in the midst of it, that her time may come, and maketh idols against herself to defile herself.

chapters.zh: 你因流了人的血，就为有罪；你做了偶像，就玷污自己，使你受报之日临近，报应之年来到。所以我叫你受列国的凌辱和列邦的讥诮。
chapters.en: Thou art become guilty in thy blood that thou hast shed; and hast defiled thyself in thine idols which thou hast made; and thou hast caused thy days to draw near, and art come unto thy years: therefore have I made thee a reproach unto the heathen, and a mocking to all countries.

chapters.zh: 你这名臭、多乱的城啊，那些离你近、离你远的都必讥诮你。
chapters.en: near, and far from thee, shall mock thee, infamous much vexed.

chapters.zh: 看哪，以色列的首领各逞其能，在你中间流人之血。
chapters.en: Behold, the princes of Israel, every one were in thee to their power to shed blood.

chapters.zh: 在你中间有轻慢父母的，有欺压寄居的，有亏负孤儿寡妇的。
chapters.en: In thee have they set light by father and mother: in the midst of thee have they dealt by oppression with the stranger: in thee have they vexed the fatherless and the widow.

chapters.zh: 你藐视了我的圣物，干犯了我的安息日。
chapters.en: Thou hast despised mine holy things, and hast profaned my sabbaths.

chapters.zh: 在你中间有谗谤人、流人血的；有在山上吃过祭偶像之物的，有行淫乱的。
chapters.en: In thee are men that carry tales to shed blood: and in thee they eat upon the mountains: in the midst of thee they commit lewdness.

chapters.zh: 在你中间有露继母下体羞辱父亲的，有玷辱月经不洁净之妇人的。
chapters.en: In thee have they discovered their fathers' nakedness: in thee have they humbled her that was set apart for pollution.

chapters.zh: 这人与邻舍的妻行可憎的事；那人贪淫玷污儿妇；还有玷辱同父之姊妹的。
chapters.en: And one hath committed abomination with his neighbour's wife; and another hath lewdly defiled his daughter in law; and another in thee hath humbled his sister, his father's daughter.

chapters.zh: 在你中间有为流人血受贿赂的；有向借钱的弟兄取利，向借粮的弟兄多要的。且因贪得无餍，欺压邻舍夺取财物，竟忘了我。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: In thee have they taken gifts to shed blood; thou hast taken usury and increase, and thou hast greedily gained of thy neighbours by extortion, and hast forgotten me, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 看哪，我因你所得不义之财和你中间所流的血，就拍掌叹息。
chapters.en: Behold, therefore I have smitten mine hand at thy dishonest gain which thou hast made, and at thy blood which hath been in the midst of thee.

chapters.zh: 到了我惩罚你的日子，你的心还能忍受么 ﹖ 你的手还能有力么 ﹖ 我 ─ 耶和华说了这话，就必照著行。
chapters.en: Can thine heart endure, or can thine hands be strong, in the days that I shall deal with thee? I the LORD have spoken , and will do .

chapters.zh: 我必将你分散在列国，四散在列邦。我也必从你中间除掉你的污秽。
chapters.en: And I will scatter thee among the heathen, and disperse thee in the countries, and will consume thy filthiness out of thee.

chapters.zh: 你必在列国人的眼前因自己所行的被亵渎，你就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And thou shalt take thine inheritance in thyself in the sight of the heathen, and thou shalt know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，以色列家在我看为渣滓。他们都是炉中的铜、锡、铁、铅，都是银渣滓。
chapters.en: Son of man, the house of Israel is to me become dross: all they brass, and tin, and iron, and lead, in the midst of the furnace; they are the dross of silver.

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：因你们都成为渣滓，我必聚集你们在耶路撒冷中。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Because ye are all become dross, behold, therefore I will gather you into the midst of Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 人怎样将银、铜、铁、铅、锡聚在炉中，吹火镕化；照样，我也要发怒气和忿怒，将你们聚集放在城中，镕化你们。
chapters.en: they gather silver, and brass, and iron, and lead, and tin, into the midst of the furnace, to blow the fire upon it, to melt ; so will I gather in mine anger and in my fury, and I will leave , and melt you.

chapters.zh: 我必聚集你们，把我烈怒的火吹在你们身上，你们就在其中镕化。
chapters.en: Yea, I will gather you, and blow upon you in the fire of my wrath, and ye shall be melted in the midst thereof.

chapters.zh: 银子怎样镕化在炉中，你们也必照样镕化在城中，你们就知道我 ─ 耶和华是将忿怒倒在你们身上了。
chapters.en: As silver is melted in the midst of the furnace, so shall ye be melted in the midst thereof; and ye shall know that I the LORD have poured out my fury upon you.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要对这地说：你是未得洁净之地，在恼恨的日子也没有雨下在你以上。
chapters.en: Son of man, say unto her, Thou the land that is not cleansed, nor rained upon in the day of indignation.

chapters.zh: 其中的先知同谋背叛，如咆哮的狮子抓撕掠物。他们吞灭人民，抢夺财宝，使这地多有寡妇。
chapters.en: a conspiracy of her prophets in the midst thereof, like a roaring lion ravening the prey; they have devoured souls; they have taken the treasure and precious things; they have made her many widows in the midst thereof.

chapters.zh: 其中的祭司强解我的律法，亵渎我的圣物，不分别圣的和俗的，也不使人分辨洁净的和不洁净的，又遮眼不顾我的安息日；我也在他们中间被亵慢。
chapters.en: Her priests have violated my law, and have profaned mine holy things: they have put no difference between the holy and profane, neither have they shewed between the unclean and the clean, and have hid their eyes from my sabbaths, and I am profaned among them.

chapters.zh: 其中的首领彷佛豺狼抓撕掠物，杀人流血，伤害人命，要得不义之财。
chapters.en: Her princes in the midst thereof like wolves ravening the prey, to shed blood, to destroy souls, to get dishonest gain.

chapters.zh: 其中的先知为百姓用未泡透的灰抹墙，就是为他们见虚假的异象，用谎诈的占卜，说：主耶和华如此说，其实耶和华没有说。
chapters.en: And her prophets have daubed them with untempered , seeing vanity, and divining lies unto them, saying, Thus saith the Lord GOD, when the LORD hath not spoken.

chapters.zh: 国内众民一味地欺压，惯行抢夺，亏负困苦穷乏的，背理欺压寄居的。
chapters.en: The people of the land have used oppression, and exercised robbery, and have vexed the poor and needy: yea, they have oppressed the stranger wrongfully.

chapters.zh: 我在他们中间寻找一人重修墙垣，在我面前为这国站在破口防堵，使我不灭绝这国，却找不著一个。
chapters.en: And I sought for a man among them, that should make up the hedge, and stand in the gap before me for the land, that I should not destroy it: but I found none.

chapters.zh: 所以我将恼恨倒在他们身上，用烈怒的火灭了他们，照他们所行的报应在他们头上。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Therefore have I poured out mine indignation upon them; I have consumed them with the fire of my wrath: their own way have I recompensed upon their heads, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: The word of the LORD came again unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，有两个女子，是一母所生，
chapters.en: Son of man, there were two women, the daughters of one mother:

chapters.zh: 他们在埃及行邪淫，在幼年时行邪淫。他们在那里作处女的时候，有人拥抱他们的怀，抚摸他们的乳。
chapters.en: And they committed whoredoms in Egypt; they committed whoredoms in their youth: there were their breasts pressed, and there they bruised the teats of their virginity.

chapters.zh: 他们的名字，姊姊名叫阿荷拉，妹妹名叫阿荷利巴。他们都归于我，生了儿女。论到他们的名字，阿荷拉就是撒玛利亚，阿荷利巴就是耶路撒冷。
chapters.en: And the names of them Aholah the elder, and Aholibah her sister: and they were mine, and they bare sons and daughters. Thus their names; Samaria Aholah, and Jerusalem Aholibah.

chapters.zh: 阿荷拉归我之后行邪淫，贪恋所爱的人，就是他的邻邦亚述人。
chapters.en: And Aholah played the harlot when she was mine; and she doted on her lovers, on the Assyrians neighbours,

chapters.zh: 这些人都穿蓝衣，作省长、副省长，都骑著马，是可爱的少年人。
chapters.en: clothed with blue, captains and rulers, all of them desirable young men, horsemen riding upon horses.

chapters.zh: 阿荷拉就与亚述人中最美的男子放纵淫行，他因所恋爱之人的一切偶像，玷污自己。
chapters.en: Thus she committed her whoredoms with them, with all them the chosen men of Assyria, and with all on whom she doted: with all their idols she defiled herself.

chapters.zh: 自从在埃及的时候，他就没有离开淫乱，因为他年幼作处女的时候，埃及人与他行淫，抚摸他的乳，纵欲与他行淫。
chapters.en: Neither left she her whoredoms from Egypt: for in her youth they lay with her, and they bruised the breasts of her virginity, and poured their whoredom upon her.

chapters.zh: 因此，我将他交在他所爱的人手中，就是他所恋爱的亚述人手中。
chapters.en: Wherefore I have delivered her into the hand of her lovers, into the hand of the Assyrians, upon whom she doted.

chapters.zh: 他们就露了他的下体，掳掠他的儿女，用刀杀了他，使他在妇女中留下臭名，因他们向他施行审判。
chapters.en: These discovered her nakedness: they took her sons and her daughters, and slew her with the sword: and she became famous among women; for they had executed judgment upon her.

chapters.zh: 他妹妹阿荷利巴虽然看见了，却还贪恋，比他姊姊更丑；行淫乱比他姊姊更多。
chapters.en: And when her sister Aholibah saw , she was more corrupt in her inordinate love than she, and in her whoredoms more than her sister in whoredoms.

chapters.zh: 他贪恋邻邦的亚述人，就是穿极华美的衣服，骑著马的省长、副省长，都是可爱的少年人。
chapters.en: She doted upon the Assyrians neighbours, captains and rulers clothed most gorgeously, horsemen riding upon horses, all of them desirable young men.

chapters.zh: 我看见他被玷污了，他姊妹二人同行一路。
chapters.en: Then I saw that she was defiled, they both one way,

chapters.zh: 阿荷利巴又加增淫行，因他看见人像画在墙上，就是用丹色所画迦勒底人的像，
chapters.en: And she increased her whoredoms: for when she saw men pourtrayed upon the wall, the images of the Chaldeans pourtrayed with vermilion,

chapters.zh: 腰间系著带子，头上有下垂的裹头巾，都是军长的形状，仿照巴比伦人的形像；他们的故土就是迦勒底。
chapters.en: Girded with girdles upon their loins, exceeding in dyed attire upon their heads, all of them princes to look to, after the manner of the Babylonians of Chaldea, the land of their nativity:

chapters.zh: 阿荷利巴一看见就贪恋他们，打发使者往迦勒底去见他们。
chapters.en: And as soon as she saw them with her eyes, she doted upon them, and sent messengers unto them into Chaldea.

chapters.zh: 巴比伦人就来登他爱情的床，与他行淫玷污他。他被玷污，随后心里与他们生疏。
chapters.en: And the Babylonians came to her into the bed of love, and they defiled her with their whoredom, and she was polluted with them, and her mind was alienated from them.

chapters.zh: 这样，他显露淫行，又显露下体；我心就与他生疏，像先前与他姊姊生疏一样。
chapters.en: So she discovered her whoredoms, and discovered her nakedness: then my mind was alienated from her, like as my mind was alienated from her sister.

chapters.zh: 他还加增他的淫行，追念他幼年在埃及地行邪淫的日子，
chapters.en: Yet she multiplied her whoredoms, in calling to remembrance the days of her youth, wherein she had played the harlot in the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 贪恋情人身壮精足，如驴如马。
chapters.en: For she doted upon their paramours, whose flesh the flesh of asses, and whose issue the issue of horses.

chapters.zh: 这样，你就想起你幼年的淫行。那时，埃及人拥抱你的怀，抚摸你的乳。
chapters.en: Thus thou calledst to remembrance the lewdness of thy youth, in bruising thy teats by the Egyptians for the paps of thy youth.

chapters.zh: 阿荷利巴啊，主耶和华如此说：我必激动你先爱而后生疏的人来攻击你。我必使他们来，在你四围攻击你。
chapters.en: Therefore, O Aholibah, thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will raise up thy lovers against thee, from whom thy mind is alienated, and I will bring them against thee on every side;

chapters.zh: 所来的就是巴比伦人、迦勒底的众人、比割人、书亚人、哥亚人，同著他们的还有亚述众人，乃是作省长、副省长、作军长有名声的，都骑著马，是可爱的少年人。
chapters.en: The Babylonians, and all the Chaldeans, Pekod, and Shoa, and Koa, all the Assyrians with them: all of them desirable young men, captains and rulers, great lords and renowned, all of them riding upon horses.

chapters.zh: 他们必带兵器、战车、辎重车，率领大众来攻击你。他们要拿大小盾牌，顶盔摆阵，在你四围攻击你。我要将审判的事交给他们，他们必按著自己的条例审判你。
chapters.en: And they shall come against thee with chariots, wagons, and wheels, and with an assembly of people, shall set against thee buckler and shield and helmet round about: and I will set judgment before them, and they shall judge thee according to their judgments.

chapters.zh: 我必以忌恨攻击你；他们必以忿怒办你。他们必割去你的鼻子和耳朵；你遗留（或译：余剩；下同）的人必倒在刀下。他们必掳去你的儿女；你所遗留的必被火焚烧。
chapters.en: And I will set my jealousy against thee, and they shall deal furiously with thee: they shall take away thy nose and thine ears; and thy remnant shall fall by the sword: they shall take thy sons and thy daughters; and thy residue shall be devoured by the fire.

chapters.zh: 他们必剥去你的衣服，夺取你华美的宝器。
chapters.en: They shall also strip thee out of thy clothes, and take away thy fair jewels.

chapters.zh: 这样，我必使你的淫行和你从埃及地染来的淫乱止息了，使你不再仰望亚述，也不再追念埃及。
chapters.en: Thus will I make thy lewdness to cease from thee, and thy whoredom from the land of Egypt: so that thou shalt not lift up thine eyes unto them, nor remember Egypt any more.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我必将你交在你所恨恶的人手中，就是你心与他生疏的人手中。
chapters.en: For thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will deliver thee into the hand whom thou hatest, into the hand from whom thy mind is alienated:

chapters.zh: 他们必以恨恶办你，夺取你一切劳碌得来的，留下你赤身露体。你淫乱的下体，连你的淫行，带你的淫乱，都被显露。
chapters.en: And they shall deal with thee hatefully, and shall take away all thy labour, and shall leave thee naked and bare: and the nakedness of thy whoredoms shall be discovered, both thy lewdness and thy whoredoms.

chapters.zh: 人必向你行这些事；因为你随从外邦人行邪淫，被他们的偶像玷污了。
chapters.en: I will do these unto thee, because thou hast gone a whoring after the heathen, because thou art polluted with their idols.

chapters.zh: 你走了你姊姊所走的路，所以我必将他的杯交在你手中。
chapters.en: Thou hast walked in the way of thy sister; therefore will I give her cup into thine hand.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：你必喝你姊姊所喝的杯；那杯又深又广，盛得甚多，使你被人嗤笑讥刺。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; Thou shalt drink of thy sister's cup deep and large: thou shalt be laughed to scorn and had in derision; it containeth much.

chapters.zh: 你必酩酊大醉，满有愁苦，喝干你姊姊撒玛利亚的杯，就是令人惊骇凄凉的杯。
chapters.en: Thou shalt be filled with drunkenness and sorrow, with the cup of astonishment and desolation, with the cup of thy sister Samaria.

chapters.zh: 你必喝这杯，以致喝尽。杯破又龈杯片，撕裂自己的乳；因为这事我曾说过。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Thou shalt even drink it and suck out, and thou shalt break the sherds thereof, and pluck off thine own breasts: for I have spoken , saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：因你忘记我，将我丢在背后，所以你要担当你淫行和淫乱的报应。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Because thou hast forgotten me, and cast me behind thy back, therefore bear thou also thy lewdness and thy whoredoms.

chapters.zh: 耶和华又对我说：人子啊，你要审问阿荷拉与阿荷利巴么 ﹖ 当指出他们所行可憎的事。
chapters.en: The LORD said moreover unto me; Son of man, wilt thou judge Aholah and Aholibah? yea, declare unto them their abominations;

chapters.zh: 他们行淫，手中有杀人的血，又与偶像行淫，并使他们为我所生的儿女经火烧给偶像。
chapters.en: That they have committed adultery, and blood in their hands, and with their idols have they committed adultery, and have also caused their sons, whom they bare unto me, to pass for them through , to devour .

chapters.zh: 此外，他们还有向我所行的，就是同日玷污我的圣所，干犯我的安息日。
chapters.en: Moreover this they have done unto me: they have defiled my sanctuary in the same day, and have profaned my sabbaths.

chapters.zh: 他们杀了儿女献与偶像，当天又入我的圣所，将圣所亵渎了。他们在我殿中所行的乃是如此。
chapters.en: For when they had slain their children to their idols, then they came the same day into my sanctuary to profane it; and, lo, thus have they done in the midst of mine house.

chapters.zh: 况且你们二妇打发使者去请远方人。使者到他们那里，他们就来了。你们为他们沐浴己身，粉饰眼目，佩戴妆饰，
chapters.en: And furthermore, that ye have sent for men to come from far, unto whom a messenger sent; and, lo, they came: for whom thou didst wash thyself, paintedst thy eyes, and deckedst thyself with ornaments,

chapters.zh: 坐在华美的床上，前面摆设桌案，将我的香料膏油摆在其上。
chapters.en: And satest upon a stately bed, and a table prepared before it, whereupon thou hast set mine incense and mine oil.

chapters.zh: 在那里有群众安逸欢乐的声音，并有粗俗的人和酒徒从旷野同来，把镯子戴在二妇的手上，把华冠戴在他们的头上。
chapters.en: And a voice of a multitude being at ease with her: and with the men of the common sort brought Sabeans from the wilderness, which put bracelets upon their hands, and beautiful crowns upon their heads.

chapters.zh: 我论这行淫衰老的妇人说：现在人还要与他行淫，他也要与人行淫。
chapters.en: Then said I unto old in adulteries, Will they now commit whoredoms with her, and she ?

chapters.zh: 人与阿荷拉，并阿荷利巴二淫妇茍合，好像与妓女苟合。
chapters.en: Yet they went in unto her, as they go in unto a woman that playeth the harlot: so went they in unto Aholah and unto Aholibah, the lewd women.

chapters.zh: 必有义人，照审判淫妇和流人血的妇人之例，审判他们；因为他们是淫妇，手中有杀人的血。
chapters.en: And the righteous men, they shall judge them after the manner of adulteresses, and after the manner of women that shed blood; because they adulteresses, and blood in their hands.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我必使多人来攻击他们，使他们抛来抛去，被人抢夺。
chapters.en: For thus saith the Lord GOD; I will bring up a company upon them, and will give them to be removed and spoiled.

chapters.zh: 这些人必用石头打死他们，用刀剑杀害他们，又杀戮他们的儿女，用火焚烧他们的房屋。
chapters.en: And the company shall stone them with stones, and dispatch them with their swords; they shall slay their sons and their daughters, and burn up their houses with fire.

chapters.zh: 这样，我必使淫行从境内止息，好叫一切妇人都受警戒，不效法你们的淫行。
chapters.en: Thus will I cause lewdness to cease out of the land, that all women may be taught not to do after your lewdness.

chapters.zh: 人必照著你们的淫行报应你们；你们要担当拜偶像的罪，就知道我是主耶和华。
chapters.en: And they shall recompense your lewdness upon you, and ye shall bear the sins of your idols: and ye shall know that I the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 第九年十月初十日，耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: Again in the ninth year, in the tenth month, in the tenth of the month, the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，今日正是巴比伦王就近耶路撒冷的日子，你要将这日记下，
chapters.en: Son of man, write thee the name of the day, of this same day: the king of Babylon set himself against Jerusalem this same day.

chapters.zh: 要向这悖逆之家设比喻说，主耶和华如此说：将锅放在火上，放好了，就倒水在其中；
chapters.en: And utter a parable unto the rebellious house, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Set on a pot, set on, and also pour water into it:

chapters.zh: 将肉块，就是一切肥美的肉块、腿，和肩都聚在其中，拿美好的骨头把锅装满；
chapters.en: Gather the pieces thereof into it, every good piece, the thigh, and the shoulder; fill with the choice bones.

chapters.zh: 取羊群中最好的，将柴堆在锅下，使锅开滚，好把骨头煮在其中。
chapters.en: Take the choice of the flock, and burn also the bones under it, make it boil well, and let them seethe the bones of it therein.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：祸哉！这流人血的城，就是长锈的锅。其中的锈未曾除掉，须要将肉块从其中一一取出来，不必为他拈阄。
chapters.en: Wherefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Woe to the bloody city, to the pot whose scum therein, and whose scum is not gone out of it! bring it out piece by piece; let no lot fall upon it.

chapters.zh: 城中所流的血倒在净光的磐石上，不倒在地上，用土掩盖。
chapters.en: For her blood is in the midst of her; she set it upon the top of a rock; she poured it not upon the ground, to cover it with dust;

chapters.zh: 这城中所流的血倒在净光的磐石上，不得掩盖，乃是出于我，为要发忿怒施行报应。
chapters.en: That it might cause fury to come up to take vengeance; I have set her blood upon the top of a rock, that it should not be covered.

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：祸哉！这流人血的城，我也必大堆火柴，
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Woe to the bloody city! I will even make the pile for fire great.

chapters.zh: 添上木柴，使火著旺，将肉煮烂，把汤熬浓，使骨头烤焦；
chapters.en: Heap on wood, kindle the fire, consume the flesh, and spice it well, and let the bones be burned.

chapters.zh: 把锅倒空坐在炭火上，使锅烧热，使铜烧红，镕化其中的污秽，除净其上的锈。
chapters.en: Then set it empty upon the coals thereof, that the brass of it may be hot, and may burn, and the filthiness of it may be molten in it, the scum of it may be consumed.

chapters.zh: 这锅劳碌疲乏，所长的大锈仍未除掉；这锈就是用火也不能除掉。
chapters.en: She hath wearied with lies, and her great scum went not forth out of her: her scum in the fire.

chapters.zh: 在你污秽中有淫行，我洁净你，你却不洁净。你的污秽再不能洁净，直等我向你发的忿怒止息。
chapters.en: In thy filthiness lewdness: because I have purged thee, and thou wast not purged, thou shalt not be purged from thy filthiness any more, till I have caused my fury to rest upon thee.

chapters.zh: 我 ─ 耶和华说过的必定成就，必照话而行，必不返回，必不顾惜，也不后悔。人必照你的举动行为审判你。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: I the LORD have spoken : it shall come to pass, and I will do ; I will not go back, neither will I spare, neither will I repent; according to thy ways, and according to thy doings, shall they judge thee, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: Also the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，我要将你眼目所喜爱的忽然取去，你却不可悲哀哭泣，也不可流泪，
chapters.en: Son of man, behold, I take away from thee the desire of thine eyes with a stroke: yet neither shalt thou mourn nor weep, neither shall thy tears run down.

chapters.zh: 只可叹息，不可出声，不可办理丧事；头上仍勒裹头巾，脚上仍穿鞋，不可蒙著嘴唇，也不可吃吊丧的食物。
chapters.en: Forbear to cry, make no mourning for the dead, bind the tire of thine head upon thee, and put on thy shoes upon thy feet, and cover not lips, and eat not the bread of men.

chapters.zh: 于是我将这事早晨告诉百姓，晚上我的妻就死了。次日早晨我便遵命而行。
chapters.en: So I spake unto the people in the morning: and at even my wife died; and I did in the morning as I was commanded.

chapters.zh: 百姓问我说：你这样行与我们有甚么关系，你不告诉我们么 ﹖
chapters.en: And the people said unto me, Wilt thou not tell us what these to us, that thou doest ?

chapters.zh: 我回答他们：耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Then I answered them, The word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 你告诉以色列家，主耶和华如此说：我必使我的圣所，就是你们势力所夸耀、眼里所喜爱、心中所爱惜的被亵渎，并且你们所遗留的儿女必倒在刀下。
chapters.en: Speak unto the house of Israel, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will profane my sanctuary, the excellency of your strength, the desire of your eyes, and that which your soul pitieth; and your sons and your daughters whom ye have left shall fall by the sword.

chapters.zh: 那时，你们必行我仆人所行的，不蒙著嘴唇，也不吃吊丧的食物。
chapters.en: And ye shall do as I have done: ye shall not cover lips, nor eat the bread of men.

chapters.zh: 你们仍要头上勒裹头巾，脚上穿鞋；不可悲哀哭泣。你们必因自己的罪孽相对叹息，渐渐消灭。
chapters.en: And your tires upon your heads, and your shoes upon your feet: ye shall not mourn nor weep; but ye shall pine away for your iniquities, and mourn one toward another.

chapters.zh: 以西结必这样为你们作预兆；凡他所行的，你们也必照样行。那事来到，你们就知道我是主耶和华。
chapters.en: Thus Ezekiel is unto you a sign: according to all that he hath done shall ye do: and when this cometh, ye shall know that I the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，我除掉他们所倚靠、所欢喜的荣耀，并眼中所喜爱、心里所重看的儿女。
chapters.en: Also, thou son of man, not in the day when I take from them their strength, the joy of their glory, the desire of their eyes, and that whereupon they set their minds, their sons and their daughters,

chapters.zh: 那日逃脱的人岂不来到你这里，使你耳闻这事么 ﹖
chapters.en: he that escapeth in that day shall come unto thee, to cause to hear with ears?

chapters.zh: 你必向逃脱的人开口说话，不再哑口。你必这样为他们作预兆，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: In that day shall thy mouth be opened to him which is escaped, and thou shalt speak, and be no more dumb: and thou shalt be a sign unto them; and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: The word of the LORD came again unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要面向亚扪人说预言，攻击他们，
chapters.en: Son of man, set thy face against the Ammonites, and prophesy against them;

chapters.zh: 说：你们当听主耶和华的话。主耶和华如此说：我的圣所被亵渎，以色列地变荒凉，犹大家被掳掠；那时，你便因这些事说：阿哈！
chapters.en: And say unto the Ammonites, Hear the word of the Lord GOD; Thus saith the Lord GOD; Because thou saidst, Aha, against my sanctuary, when it was profaned; and against the land of Israel, when it was desolate; and against the house of Judah, when they went into captivity;

chapters.zh: 所以我必将你的地交给东方人为业；他们必在你的地上安营居住，吃你的果子，喝你的奶。
chapters.en: Behold, therefore I will deliver thee to the men of the east for a possession, and they shall set their palaces in thee, and make their dwellings in thee: they shall eat thy fruit, and they shall drink thy milk.

chapters.zh: 我必使拉巴为骆驼场，使亚扪人的地为羊群躺卧之处，你们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And I will make Rabbah a stable for camels, and the Ammonites a couchingplace for flocks: and ye shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：因你拍手顿足，以满心的恨恶向以色列地欢喜，
chapters.en: For thus saith the Lord GOD; Because thou hast clapped hands, and stamped with the feet, and rejoiced in heart with all thy despite against the land of Israel;

chapters.zh: 所以我伸手攻击你，将你交给列国作为掳物。我必从万民中剪除你，使你从万国中败亡。我必除灭你，你就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Behold, therefore I will stretch out mine hand upon thee, and will deliver thee for a spoil to the heathen; and I will cut thee off from the people, and I will cause thee to perish out of the countries: I will destroy thee; and thou shalt know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：因摩押和西珥人说：看哪，犹大家与列国无异，
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; Because that Moab and Seir do say, Behold, the house of Judah like unto all the heathen;

chapters.zh: 所以我要破开摩押边界上的城邑，就是摩押人看为本国之荣耀的伯耶西末、巴力免、基列亭，
chapters.en: Therefore, behold, I will open the side of Moab from the cities, from his cities on his frontiers, the glory of the country, Bethjeshimoth, Baalmeon, and Kiriathaim,

chapters.zh: 好使东方人来攻击亚扪人。我必将亚扪人之地交给他们为业，使亚扪人在列国中不再被记念。
chapters.en: Unto the men of the east with the Ammonites, and will give them in possession, that the Ammonites may not be remembered among the nations.

chapters.zh: 我必向摩押施行审判，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And I will execute judgments upon Moab; and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：因为以东报仇雪恨，攻击犹大家，向他们报仇，大大有罪，
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; Because that Edom hath dealt against the house of Judah by taking vengeance, and hath greatly offended, and revenged himself upon them;

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：我必伸手攻击以东，剪除人与牲畜，使以东从提幔起，人必倒在刀下，地要变为荒凉，直到底但。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; I will also stretch out mine hand upon Edom, and will cut off man and beast from it; and I will make it desolate from Teman; and they of Dedan shall fall by the sword.

chapters.zh: 我必藉我民以色列的手报复以东；以色列民必照我的怒气，按我的忿怒在以东施报，以东人就知道是我施报。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And I will lay my vengeance upon Edom by the hand of my people Israel: and they shall do in Edom according to mine anger and according to my fury; and they shall know my vengeance, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：因非利士人向犹大人报仇，就是以恨恶的心报仇雪恨，永怀仇恨，要毁灭他们，
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; Because the Philistines have dealt by revenge, and have taken vengeance with a despiteful heart, to destroy for the old hatred;

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：我必伸手攻击非利士人，剪除基利提人，灭绝沿海剩下的居民。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will stretch out mine hand upon the Philistines, and I will cut off the Cherethims, and destroy the remnant of the sea coast.

chapters.zh: 我向他们大施报应，发怒斥责他们。我报复他们的时候，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And I will execute great vengeance upon them with furious rebukes; and they shall know that I the LORD, when I shall lay my vengeance upon them.

chapters.zh: 第十一年十一月初一日，耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the eleventh year, in the first of the month, the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，因推罗向耶路撒冷说：阿哈，那作众民之门的已经破坏，向我开放；他既变为荒场，我必丰盛。
chapters.en: Son of man, because that Tyrus hath said against Jerusalem, Aha, she is broken the gates of the people: she is turned unto me: I shall be replenished, she is laid waste:

chapters.zh: 所以，主耶和华如此说：推罗啊，我必与你为敌，使许多国民上来攻击你，如同海使波浪涌上来一样。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I against thee, O Tyrus, and will cause many nations to come up against thee, as the sea causeth his waves to come up.

chapters.zh: 他们必破坏推罗的墙垣，拆毁他的城楼。我也要刮净尘土，使他成为净光的磐石。
chapters.en: And they shall destroy the walls of Tyrus, and break down her towers: I will also scrape her dust from her, and make her like the top of a rock.

chapters.zh: 他必在海中作晒网的地方，也必成为列国的掳物。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: It shall be the spreading of nets in the midst of the sea: for I have spoken , saith the Lord GOD: and it shall become a spoil to the nations.

chapters.zh: 属推罗城邑的居民（原文是田间的众女；八节同）必被刀剑杀灭，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And her daughters which in the field shall be slain by the sword; and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我必使诸王之王的巴比伦王尼布甲尼撒率领马匹车辆、马兵、军队，和许多人民从北方来攻击你推罗。
chapters.en: For thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will bring upon Tyrus Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, a king of kings, from the north, with horses, and with chariots, and with horsemen, and companies, and much people.

chapters.zh: 他必用刀剑杀灭属你城邑的居民，也必造台筑垒举盾牌攻击你。
chapters.en: He shall slay with the sword thy daughters in the field: and he shall make a fort against thee, and cast a mount against thee, and lift up the buckler against thee.

chapters.zh: 他必安设撞城锤攻破你的墙垣，用铁器拆毁你的城楼。
chapters.en: And he shall set engines of war against thy walls, and with his axes he shall break down thy towers.

chapters.zh: 因他的马匹众多，尘土扬起遮蔽你。他进入你的城门，好像人进入已有破口之城。那时，你的墙垣必因骑马的和战车、辎重车的响声震动。
chapters.en: By reason of the abundance of his horses their dust shall cover thee: thy walls shall shake at the noise of the horsemen, and of the wheels, and of the chariots, when he shall enter into thy gates, as men enter into a city wherein is made a breach.

chapters.zh: 他的马蹄必践踏你一切的街道，他必用刀杀戮你的居民；你坚固的柱子（或译：柱像）必倒在地上。
chapters.en: With the hoofs of his horses shall he tread down all thy streets: he shall slay thy people by the sword, and thy strong garrisons shall go down to the ground.

chapters.zh: 人必以你的财宝为掳物，以你的货财为掠物，破坏你的墙垣，拆毁你华美的房屋，将你的石头、木头、尘土都抛在水中。
chapters.en: And they shall make a spoil of thy riches, and make a prey of thy merchandise: and they shall break down thy walls, and destroy thy pleasant houses: and they shall lay thy stones and thy timber and thy dust in the midst of the water.

chapters.zh: 我必使你唱歌的声音止息，人也不再听见你弹琴的声音。
chapters.en: And I will cause the noise of thy songs to cease; and the sound of thy harps shall be no more heard.

chapters.zh: 我必使你成为净光的磐石，作晒网的地方；你不得再被建造，因为这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And I will make thee like the top of a rock: thou shalt be to spread nets upon; thou shalt be built no more: for I the LORD have spoken , saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华对推罗如此说：在你中间行杀戮，受伤之人唉哼的时候，因你倾倒的响声，海岛岂不都震动么 ﹖
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD to Tyrus; Shall not the isles shake at the sound of thy fall, when the wounded cry, when the slaughter is made in the midst of thee?

chapters.zh: 那时靠海的君王必都下位，除去朝服，脱下花衣，披上战兢，坐在地上，时刻发抖，为你惊骇。
chapters.en: Then all the princes of the sea shall come down from their thrones, and lay away their robes, and put off their broidered garments: they shall clothe themselves with trembling; they shall sit upon the ground, and shall tremble at moment, and be astonished at thee.

chapters.zh: 他们必为你作起哀歌说：你这有名之城，素为航海之人居住，在海上为最坚固的；平日你和居民使一切住在那里的人无不惊恐；现在何竟毁灭了 ﹖
chapters.en: And they shall take up a lamentation for thee, and say to thee, How art thou destroyed, inhabited of seafaring men, the renowned city, which wast strong in the sea, she and her inhabitants, which cause their terror on all that haunt it!

chapters.zh: 如今在你这倾覆的日子，海岛都必战兢；海中的群岛见你归于无有就都惊惶。
chapters.en: Now shall the isles tremble in the day of thy fall; yea, the isles that in the sea shall be troubled at thy departure.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：推罗啊，我使你变为荒凉，如无人居住的城邑；又使深水漫过你，大水淹没你。
chapters.en: For thus saith the Lord GOD; When I shall make thee a desolate city, like the cities that are not inhabited; when I shall bring up the deep upon thee, and great waters shall cover thee;

chapters.zh: 那时，我要叫你下入阴府，与古时的人一同在地的深处、久已荒凉之地居住，使你不再有居民。我也要在活人之地显荣耀（我也 … … 荣耀：或译在活人之地不再有荣耀）。
chapters.en: When I shall bring thee down with them that descend into the pit, with the people of old time, and shall set thee in the low parts of the earth, in places desolate of old, with them that go down to the pit, that thou be not inhabited; and I shall set glory in the land of the living;

chapters.zh: 我必叫你令人惊恐，不再存留于世；人虽寻找你，却永寻不见。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: I will make thee a terror, and thou no : though thou be sought for, yet shalt thou never be found again, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: The word of the LORD came again unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，要为推罗作起哀歌，
chapters.en: Now, thou son of man, take up a lamentation for Tyrus;

chapters.zh: 说：你居住海口，是众民的商埠；你的交易通到许多的海岛。主耶和华如此说：推罗啊，你曾说：我是全然美丽的。
chapters.en: And say unto Tyrus, O thou that art situate at the entry of the sea, a merchant of the people for many isles, Thus saith the Lord GOD; O Tyrus, thou hast said, I of perfect beauty.

chapters.zh: 你的境界在海中，造你的使你全然美丽。
chapters.en: Thy borders in the midst of the seas, thy builders have perfected thy beauty.

chapters.zh: 他们用示尼珥的松树做你的一切板，用利巴嫩的香柏树做桅杆，
chapters.en: They have made all thy boards of fir trees of Senir: they have taken cedars from Lebanon to make masts for thee.

chapters.zh: 用巴珊的橡树做你的桨，用象牙镶嵌基提海岛的黄杨木为坐板（或译：舱板）。
chapters.en: the oaks of Bashan have they made thine oars; the company of the Ashurites have made thy benches ivory, out of the isles of Chittim.

chapters.zh: 你的篷帆是用埃及绣花细麻布做的，可以做你的大旗；你的凉棚是用以利沙岛的蓝色、紫色布做的。
chapters.en: Fine linen with broidered work from Egypt was that which thou spreadest forth to be thy sail; blue and purple from the isles of Elishah was that which covered thee.

chapters.zh: 西顿和亚发的居民作你荡桨的。推罗啊，你中间的智慧人作掌舵的。
chapters.en: The inhabitants of Zidon and Arvad were thy mariners: thy wise , O Tyrus, were in thee, were thy pilots.

chapters.zh: 迦巴勒的老者和聪明人都在你中间作补缝的；一切泛海的船只和水手都在你中间经营交易的事。
chapters.en: The ancients of Gebal and the wise thereof were in thee thy calkers: all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in thee to occupy thy merchandise.

chapters.zh: 波斯人、路德人、弗人在你军营中作战士；他们在你中间悬挂盾牌和头盔，彰显你的尊荣。
chapters.en: They of Persia and of Lud and of Phut were in thine army, thy men of war: they hanged the shield and helmet in thee; they set forth thy comeliness.

chapters.zh: 亚发人和你的军队都在你四围的墙上，你的望楼也有勇士；他们悬挂盾牌，成全你的美丽。
chapters.en: The men of Arvad with thine army upon thy walls round about, and the Gammadims were in thy towers: they hanged their shields upon thy walls round about; they have made thy beauty perfect.

chapters.zh: 他施人因你多有各类的财物，就作你的客商，拿银、铁、锡、铅兑换你的货物。
chapters.en: Tarshish thy merchant by reason of the multitude of all riches; with silver, iron, tin, and lead, they traded in thy fairs.

chapters.zh: 雅完人、土巴人、米设人都与你交易；他们用人口和铜器兑换你的货物。
chapters.en: Javan, Tubal, and Meshech, they thy merchants: they traded the persons of men and vessels of brass in thy market.

chapters.zh: 陀迦玛族用马和战马并骡子兑换你的货物。
chapters.en: They of the house of Togarmah traded in thy fairs with horses and horsemen and mules.

chapters.zh: 底但人与你交易，许多海岛作你的码头；他们拿象牙、乌木与你兑换（或译：进贡）。
chapters.en: The men of Dedan thy merchants; many isles the merchandise of thine hand: they brought thee a present horns of ivory and ebony.

chapters.zh: 亚兰人因你的工作很多，就作你的客商；他们用绿宝石、紫色布绣货、细麻布、珊瑚、红宝石兑换你的货物。
chapters.en: Syria thy merchant by reason of the multitude of the wares of thy making: they occupied in thy fairs with emeralds, purple, and broidered work, and fine linen, and coral, and agate.

chapters.zh: 犹大和以色列地的人都与你交易；他们用米匿的麦子、饼、蜜、油、乳香兑换你的货物。
chapters.en: Judah, and the land of Israel, they thy merchants: they traded in thy market wheat of Minnith, and Pannag, and honey, and oil, and balm.

chapters.zh: 大马色人因你的工作很多，又因你多有各类的财物，就拿黑本酒和白羊毛与你交易。
chapters.en: Damascus thy merchant in the multitude of the wares of thy making, for the multitude of all riches; in the wine of Helbon, and white wool.

chapters.zh: 威但人和雅完人拿纺成的线、亮铁、桂皮、菖蒲兑换你的货物。
chapters.en: Dan also and Javan going to and fro occupied in thy fairs: bright iron, cassia, and calamus, were in thy market.

chapters.zh: 底但人用高贵的毯子、鞍、屉与你交易。
chapters.en: Dedan thy merchant in precious clothes for chariots.

chapters.zh: 亚拉伯人和基达的一切首领都作你的客商，用羊羔、公绵羊、公山羊与你交易。
chapters.en: Arabia, and all the princes of Kedar, they occupied with thee in lambs, and rams, and goats: in these thy merchants.

chapters.zh: 示巴和拉玛的商人与你交易，他们用各类上好的香料、各类的宝石，和黄金兑换你的货物。
chapters.en: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah, they thy merchants: they occupied in thy fairs with chief of all spices, and with all precious stones, and gold.

chapters.zh: 哈兰人、干尼人、伊甸人、示巴的商人，和亚述人、基抹人与你交易。
chapters.en: Haran, and Canneh, and Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, Chilmad, thy merchants.

chapters.zh: 这些商人以美好的货物包在绣花蓝色包袱内，又有华丽的衣服装在香柏木的箱子里，用绳捆著与你交易。
chapters.en: These thy merchants in all sorts , in blue clothes, and broidered work, and in chests of rich apparel, bound with cords, and made of cedar, among thy merchandise.

chapters.zh: 他施的船只接连成帮为你运货，你便在海中丰富极其荣华。
chapters.en: The ships of Tarshish did sing of thee in thy market: and thou wast replenished, and made very glorious in the midst of the seas.

chapters.zh: 荡桨的已经把你荡到大水之处，东风在海中将你打破。
chapters.en: Thy rowers have brought thee into great waters: the east wind hath broken thee in the midst of the seas.

chapters.zh: 你的资财、物件、货物、水手、掌舵的、补缝的、经营交易的，并你中间的战士和人民，在你破坏的日子必都沉在海中。
chapters.en: Thy riches, and thy fairs, thy merchandise, thy mariners, and thy pilots, thy calkers, and the occupiers of thy merchandise, and all thy men of war, that in thee, and in all thy company which in the midst of thee, shall fall into the midst of the seas in the day of thy ruin.

chapters.zh: 你掌舵的呼号之声一发，郊野都必震动。
chapters.en: The suburbs shall shake at the sound of the cry of thy pilots.

chapters.zh: 凡荡桨的和水手，并一切泛海掌舵的，都必下船登岸。
chapters.en: And all that handle the oar, the mariners, all the pilots of the sea, shall come down from their ships, they shall stand upon the land;

chapters.zh: 他们必为你放声痛哭，把尘土撒在头上，在灰中打滚；
chapters.en: And shall cause their voice to be heard against thee, and shall cry bitterly, and shall cast up dust upon their heads, they shall wallow themselves in the ashes:

chapters.zh: 又为你使头上光秃，用麻布束腰，号咷痛哭，苦苦悲哀。
chapters.en: And they shall make themselves utterly bald for thee, and gird them with sackcloth, and they shall weep for thee with bitterness of heart bitter wailing.

chapters.zh: 他们哀号的时候，为你作起哀歌哀哭，说：有何城如推罗 ﹖ 有何城如他在海中成为寂寞的呢 ﹖
chapters.en: And in their wailing they shall take up a lamentation for thee, and lament over thee, , What like Tyrus, like the destroyed in the midst of the sea?

chapters.zh: 你由海上运出货物，就使许多国民充足；你以许多资财、货物使地上的君王丰富。
chapters.en: When thy wares went forth out of the seas, thou filledst many people; thou didst enrich the kings of the earth with the multitude of thy riches and of thy merchandise.

chapters.zh: 你在深水中被海浪打破的时候，你的货物和你中间的一切人民，就都沉下去了。
chapters.en: In the time thou shalt be broken by the seas in the depths of the waters thy merchandise and all thy company in the midst of thee shall fall.

chapters.zh: 海岛的居民为你惊奇；他们的君王都甚恐慌，面带愁容。
chapters.en: All the inhabitants of the isles shall be astonished at thee, and their kings shall be sore afraid, they shall be troubled in countenance.

chapters.zh: 各国民中的客商都向你发嘶声；你令人惊恐，不再存留于世，直到永远。
chapters.en: The merchants among the people shall hiss at thee; thou shalt be a terror, and never any more.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: The word of the LORD came again unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你对推罗君王说，主耶和华如此说：因你心里高傲，说：我是神；我在海中坐神之位。你虽然居心自比神，也不过是人，并不是神！
chapters.en: Son of man, say unto the prince of Tyrus, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Because thine heart lifted up, and thou hast said, I a God, I sit the seat of God, in the midst of the seas; yet thou a man, and not God, though thou set thine heart as the heart of God:

chapters.zh: 看哪，你比但以理更有智慧，甚么秘事都不能向你隐藏。
chapters.en: Behold, thou wiser than Daniel; there is no secret that they can hide from thee:

chapters.zh: 你靠自己的智慧聪明得了金银财宝，收入库中。
chapters.en: With thy wisdom and with thine understanding thou hast gotten thee riches, and hast gotten gold and silver into thy treasures:

chapters.zh: 你靠自己的大智慧和贸易增添资财，又因资财心里高傲。
chapters.en: By thy great wisdom by thy traffick hast thou increased thy riches, and thine heart is lifted up because of thy riches:

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：因你居心自比神，
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Because thou hast set thine heart as the heart of God;

chapters.zh: 我必使外邦人，就是列国中的强暴人临到你这里；他们必拔刀砍坏你用智慧得来的美物，亵渎你的荣光。
chapters.en: Behold, therefore I will bring strangers upon thee, the terrible of the nations: and they shall draw their swords against the beauty of thy wisdom, and they shall defile thy brightness.

chapters.zh: 他们必使你下坑；你必死在海中，与被杀的人一样。
chapters.en: They shall bring thee down to the pit, and thou shalt die the deaths of slain in the midst of the seas.

chapters.zh: 在杀你的人面前你还能说我是神么 ﹖ 其实你在杀害你的人手中，不过是人，并不是神。
chapters.en: Wilt thou yet say before him that slayeth thee, I God? but thou a man, and no God, in the hand of him that slayeth thee.

chapters.zh: 你必死在外邦人手中，与未受割礼（或译：不洁；下同）的人一样，因为这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Thou shalt die the deaths of the uncircumcised by the hand of strangers: for I have spoken , saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Moreover the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你为推罗王作起哀歌，说主耶和华如此说：你无所不备，智慧充足，全然美丽。
chapters.en: Son of man, take up a lamentation upon the king of Tyrus, and say unto him, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Thou sealest up the sum, full of wisdom, and perfect in beauty.

chapters.zh: 你曾在伊甸神的园中，佩戴各样宝石，就是红宝石、红璧玺、金钢石、水苍玉、红玛瑙、碧玉、蓝宝石、绿宝石、红玉，和黄金；又有精美的鼓笛在你那里，都是在你受造之日预备齐全的。
chapters.en: Thou hast been in Eden the garden of God; every precious stone thy covering, the sardius, topaz, and the diamond, the beryl, the onyx, and the jasper, the sapphire, the emerald, and the carbuncle, and gold: the workmanship of thy tabrets and of thy pipes was prepared in thee in the day that thou wast created.

chapters.zh: 你是那受膏遮掩约柜的基路伯；我将你安置在神的圣山上；你在发光如火的宝石中间往来。
chapters.en: Thou the anointed cherub that covereth; and I have set thee : thou wast upon the holy mountain of God; thou hast walked up and down in the midst of the stones of fire.

chapters.zh: 你从受造之日所行的都完全，后来在你中间又察出不义。
chapters.en: Thou perfect in thy ways from the day that thou wast created, till iniquity was found in thee.

chapters.zh: 因你贸易很多，就被强暴的事充满，以致犯罪，所以我因你亵渎圣地，就从神的山驱逐你。遮掩约柜的基路伯啊，我已将你从发光如火的宝石中除灭。
chapters.en: By the multitude of thy merchandise they have filled the midst of thee with violence, and thou hast sinned: therefore I will cast thee as profane out of the mountain of God: and I will destroy thee, O covering cherub, from the midst of the stones of fire.

chapters.zh: 你因美丽心中高傲，又因荣光败坏智慧，我已将你摔倒在地，使你倒在君王面前，好叫他们目睹眼见。
chapters.en: Thine heart was lifted up because of thy beauty, thou hast corrupted thy wisdom by reason of thy brightness: I will cast thee to the ground, I will lay thee before kings, that they may behold thee.

chapters.zh: 你因罪孽众多，贸易不公，就亵渎你那里的圣所。故此，我使火从你中间发出，烧灭你，使你在所有观看的人眼前变为地上的炉灰。
chapters.en: Thou hast defiled thy sanctuaries by the multitude of thine iniquities, by the iniquity of thy traffick; therefore will I bring forth a fire from the midst of thee, it shall devour thee, and I will bring thee to ashes upon the earth in the sight of all them that behold thee.

chapters.zh: 各国民中，凡认识你的，都必为你惊奇。你令人惊恐，不再存留于世，直到永远。
chapters.en: All they that know thee among the people shall be astonished at thee: thou shalt be a terror, and never thou any more.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Again the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要向西顿预言攻击他，
chapters.en: Son of man, set thy face against Zidon, and prophesy against it,

chapters.zh: 说主耶和华如此说：西顿哪，我与你为敌，我必在你中间得荣耀。我在你中间施行审判、显为圣的时候，人就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And say, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I against thee, O Zidon; and I will be glorified in the midst of thee: and they shall know that I the LORD, when I shall have executed judgments in her, and shall be sanctified in her.

chapters.zh: 我必使瘟疫进入西顿，使血流在他街上。被杀的必在其中仆倒，四围有刀剑临到他，人就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: For I will send into her pestilence, and blood into her streets; and the wounded shall be judged in the midst of her by the sword upon her on every side; and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 四围恨恶以色列家的人，必不再向他们作刺人的荆棘，伤人的蒺藜，人就知道我是主耶和华。
chapters.en: And there shall be no more a pricking brier unto the house of Israel, nor grieving thorn of all round about them, that despised them; and they shall know that I the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我将分散在万民中的以色列家招聚回来，向他们在列邦人眼前显为圣的时候，他们就在我赐给我仆人雅各之地，仍然居住。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; When I shall have gathered the house of Israel from the people among whom they are scattered, and shall be sanctified in them in the sight of the heathen, then shall they dwell in their land that I have given to my servant Jacob.

chapters.zh: 他们要在这地上安然居住。我向四围恨恶他们的众人施行审判以后，他们要盖造房屋，栽种葡萄园，安然居住，就知道我是耶和华 ─ 他们的神。
chapters.en: And they shall dwell safely therein, and shall build houses, and plant vineyards; yea, they shall dwell with confidence, when I have executed judgments upon all those that despise them round about them; and they shall know that I the LORD their God.

chapters.zh: 第十年十月十二日，耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: In the tenth year, in the tenth , in the twelfth of the month, the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要向埃及王法老预言攻击他和埃及全地，
chapters.en: Son of man, set thy face against Pharaoh king of Egypt, and prophesy against him, and against all Egypt:

chapters.zh: 说主耶和华如此说：埃及王法老啊，我与你这卧在自己河中的大鱼为敌。你曾说：这河是我的，是我为自己造的。
chapters.en: Speak, and say, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I against thee, Pharaoh king of Egypt, the great dragon that lieth in the midst of his rivers, which hath said, My river mine own, and I have made for myself.

chapters.zh: 我 ─ 耶和华必用钩子钩住你的腮颊，又使江河中的鱼贴住你的鳞甲；我必将你和所有贴住你鳞甲的鱼，从江河中拉上来，
chapters.en: But I will put hooks in thy jaws, and I will cause the fish of thy rivers to stick unto thy scales, and I will bring thee up out of the midst of thy rivers, and all the fish of thy rivers shall stick unto thy scales.

chapters.zh: 把你并江河中的鱼都抛在旷野；你必倒在田间，不被收殓，不被掩埋。我已将你给地上野兽、空中飞鸟作食物。
chapters.en: And I will leave thee into the wilderness, thee and all the fish of thy rivers: thou shalt fall upon the open fields; thou shalt not be brought together, nor gathered: I have given thee for meat to the beasts of the field and to the fowls of the heaven.

chapters.zh: 埃及一切的居民，因向以色列家成了芦苇的杖，就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And all the inhabitants of Egypt shall know that I the LORD, because they have been a staff of reed to the house of Israel.

chapters.zh: 他们用手持住你，你就断折，伤了他们的肩；他们倚靠你，你就断折，闪了他们的腰。
chapters.en: When they took hold of thee by thy hand, thou didst break, and rend all their shoulder: and when they leaned upon thee, thou brakest, and madest all their loins to be at a stand.

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：我必使刀剑临到你，从你中间将人与牲畜剪除。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will bring a sword upon thee, and cut off man and beast out of thee.

chapters.zh: 埃及地必荒废凄凉，他们就知道我是耶和华。因为法老说：这河是我的，是我所造的，
chapters.en: And the land of Egypt shall be desolate and waste; and they shall know that I the LORD: because he hath said, The river mine, and I have made .

chapters.zh: 所以我必与你并你的江河为敌，使埃及地，从色弗尼塔直到古实境界，全然荒废凄凉。
chapters.en: Behold, therefore I against thee, and against thy rivers, and I will make the land of Egypt utterly waste desolate, from the tower of Syene even unto the border of Ethiopia.

chapters.zh: 人的脚、兽的蹄都不经过，四十年之久并无人居住。
chapters.en: No foot of man shall pass through it, nor foot of beast shall pass through it, neither shall it be inhabited forty years.

chapters.zh: 我必使埃及地在荒凉的国中成为荒凉，使埃及城在荒废的城中变为荒废，共有四十年。我必将埃及人分散在列国，四散在列邦。
