chapters.en: And I will make the land of Egypt desolate in the midst of the countries desolate, and her cities among the cities laid waste shall be desolate forty years: and I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations, and will disperse them through the countries.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：满了四十年，我必招聚分散在各国民中的埃及人。
chapters.en: Yet thus saith the Lord GOD; At the end of forty years will I gather the Egyptians from the people whither they were scattered:

chapters.zh: 我必叫埃及被掳的人回来，使他们归回本地巴忒罗。在那里必成为低微的国，
chapters.en: And I will bring again the captivity of Egypt, and will cause them to return the land of Pathros, into the land of their habitation; and they shall be there a base kingdom.

chapters.zh: 必为列国中最低微的，也不再自高于列国之上。我必减少他们，以致不再辖制列国。
chapters.en: It shall be the basest of the kingdoms; neither shall it exalt itself any more above the nations: for I will diminish them, that they shall no more rule over the nations.

chapters.zh: 埃及必不再作以色列家所倚靠的；以色列家仰望埃及人的时候，便思念罪孽。他们就知道我是主耶和华。
chapters.en: And it shall be no more the confidence of the house of Israel, which bringeth iniquity to remembrance, when they shall look after them: but they shall know that I the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 二十七年正月初一日，耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the seven and twentieth year, in the first , in the first of the month, the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，巴比伦王尼布甲尼撒使他的军兵大大效劳，攻打推罗，以致头都光秃，肩都磨破；然而他和他的军兵攻打推罗，并没有从那里得甚么酬劳。
chapters.en: Son of man, Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon caused his army to serve a great service against Tyrus: every head made bald, and every shoulder peeled: yet had he no wages, nor his army, for Tyrus, for the service that he had served against it:

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：我必将埃及地赐给巴比伦王尼布甲尼撒；他必掳掠埃及群众，抢其中的财为掳物，夺其中的货为掠物，这就可以作他军兵的酬劳。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will give the land of Egypt unto Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon; and he shall take her multitude, and take her spoil, and take her prey; and it shall be the wages for his army.

chapters.zh: 我将埃及地赐给他，酬他所效的劳，因王与军兵是为我勤劳。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: I have given him the land of Egypt his labour wherewith he served against it, because they wrought for me, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 当那日，我必使以色列家的角发生，又必使你 ─ 以西结在他们中间得以开口；他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: In that day will I cause the horn of the house of Israel to bud forth, and I will give thee the opening of the mouth in the midst of them; and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: The word of the LORD came again unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要发预言说，主耶和华如此说：哀哉这日！你们应当哭号。
chapters.en: Son of man, prophesy and say, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Howl ye, Woe worth the day!

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华的日子临近，就是密云之日，列国受罚之期。
chapters.en: For the day near, even the day of the LORD near, a cloudy day; it shall be the time of the heathen.

chapters.zh: 必有刀剑临到埃及；在埃及被杀之人仆倒的时候，古实人就有痛苦，人民必被掳掠，基址必被拆毁。
chapters.en: And the sword shall come upon Egypt, and great pain shall be in Ethiopia, when the slain shall fall in Egypt, and they shall take away her multitude, and her foundations shall be broken down.

chapters.zh: 古实人、弗人（或译：吕彼亚）、路德人、杂族的人民，并古巴人，以及同盟之地的人都要与埃及人一同倒在刀下。
chapters.en: Ethiopia, and Libya, and Lydia, and all the mingled people, and Chub, and the men of the land that is in league, shall fall with them by the sword.

chapters.zh: 耶和华如此说：扶助埃及的也必倾倒。埃及因势力而有的骄傲必降低微；其中的人民，从色弗尼塔起（见二十九章十节）必倒在刀下。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD; They also that uphold Egypt shall fall; and the pride of her power shall come down: from the tower of Syene shall they fall in it by the sword, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 埃及地在荒凉的国中必成为荒凉；埃及城在荒废的城中也变为荒废。
chapters.en: And they shall be desolate in the midst of the countries desolate, and her cities shall be in the midst of the cities wasted.

chapters.zh: 我在埃及中使火著起；帮助埃及的，都被灭绝。那时，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And they shall know that I the LORD, when I have set a fire in Egypt, and all her helpers shall be destroyed.

chapters.zh: 到那日，必有使者坐船，从我面前出去，使安逸无虑的古实人惊惧；必有痛苦临到他们，好像埃及遭灾的日子一样。看哪，这事临近了！
chapters.en: In that day shall messengers go forth from me in ships to make the careless Ethiopians afraid, and great pain shall come upon them, as in the day of Egypt: for, lo, it cometh.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我必藉巴比伦王尼布甲尼撒的手，除灭埃及众人。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; I will also make the multitude of Egypt to cease by the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon.

chapters.zh: 他和随从他的人，就是列国中强暴的，必进来毁灭这地。他们必拔刀攻击埃及，使遍地有被杀的人。
chapters.en: He and his people with him, the terrible of the nations, shall be brought to destroy the land: and they shall draw their swords against Egypt, and fill the land with the slain.

chapters.zh: 我必使江河干涸，将地卖在恶人的手中；我必藉外邦人的手，使这地和其中所有的变为凄凉。这是我 ─ 耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And I will make the rivers dry, and sell the land into the hand of the wicked: and I will make the land waste, and all that is therein, by the hand of strangers: I the LORD have spoken .

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我必毁灭偶像，从挪弗除灭神像；必不再有君王出自埃及地。我要使埃及地的人惧怕。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; I will also destroy the idols, and I will cause images to cease out of Noph; and there shall be no more a prince of the land of Egypt: and I will put a fear in the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 我必使巴忒罗荒凉，在琐安中使火著起，向挪施行审判。
chapters.en: And I will make Pathros desolate, and will set fire in Zoan, and will execute judgments in No.

chapters.zh: 我必将我的忿怒倒在埃及的保障上，就是训上，并要剪除挪的众人。
chapters.en: And I will pour my fury upon Sin, the strength of Egypt; and I will cut off the multitude of No.

chapters.zh: 我必在埃及中使火著起；训必大大痛苦；挪必被攻破；挪弗白日（或译：终日）见仇敌。
chapters.en: And I will set fire in Egypt: Sin shall have great pain, and No shall be rent asunder, and Noph distresses daily.

chapters.zh: 亚文和比伯实的少年人必倒在刀下；这些城的人必被掳掠。
chapters.en: The young men of Aven and of Pibeseth shall fall by the sword: and these shall go into captivity.

chapters.zh: 我在答比匿折断埃及的诸轭，使他因势力而有的骄傲在其中止息。那时，日光必退去；至于这城，必有密云遮蔽，其中的女子必被掳掠。
chapters.en: At Tehaphnehes also the day shall be darkened, when I shall break there the yokes of Egypt: and the pomp of her strength shall cease in her: as for her, a cloud shall cover her, and her daughters shall go into captivity.

chapters.zh: 我必这样向埃及施行审判，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Thus will I execute judgments in Egypt: and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 十一年正月初七日，耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the eleventh year, in the first , in the seventh of the month, the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，我已打折埃及王法老的膀臂；没有敷药，也没有用布缠好，使他有力持刀。
chapters.en: Son of man, I have broken the arm of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and, lo, it shall not be bound up to be healed, to put a roller to bind it, to make it strong to hold the sword.

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：看哪，我与埃及王法老为敌，必将他有力的膀臂和已打折的膀臂全行打断，使刀从他手中坠落。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I against Pharaoh king of Egypt, and will break his arms, the strong, and that which was broken; and I will cause the sword to fall out of his hand.

chapters.zh: 我必将埃及人分散在列国，四散在列邦。
chapters.en: And I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations, and will disperse them through the countries.

chapters.zh: 我必使巴比伦王的膀臂有力，将我的刀交在他手中；却要打断法老的膀臂，他就在巴比伦王面前唉哼，如同受死伤的人一样。
chapters.en: And I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon, and put my sword in his hand: but I will break Pharaoh's arms, and he shall groan before him with the groanings of a deadly wounded .

chapters.zh: 我必扶持巴比伦王的膀臂，法老的膀臂却要下垂；我将我的刀交在巴比伦王手中，他必举刀攻击埃及地，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: But I will strengthen the arms of the king of Babylon, and the arms of Pharaoh shall fall down; and they shall know that I the LORD, when I shall put my sword into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall stretch it out upon the land of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 我必将埃及人分散在列国，四散在列邦；他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And I will scatter the Egyptians among the nations, and disperse them among the countries; and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 十一年三月初一日，耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the eleventh year, in the third , in the first of the month, the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要向埃及王法老和他的众人说：在威势上谁能与你相比呢？
chapters.en: Son of man, speak unto Pharaoh king of Egypt, and to his multitude; Whom art thou like in thy greatness?

chapters.zh: 亚述王曾如利巴嫩中的香柏树，枝条荣美，影密如林，极其高大，树尖插入云中。
chapters.en: Behold, the Assyrian a cedar in Lebanon with fair branches, and with a shadowing shroud, and of an high stature; and his top was among the thick boughs.

chapters.zh: 众水使他生长；深水使他长大。所栽之地有江河围流，汊出的水道延到田野诸树。
chapters.en: The waters made him great, the deep set him up on high with her rivers running round about his plants, and sent out her little rivers unto all the trees of the field.

chapters.zh: 所以他高大超过田野诸树；发旺的时候，枝子繁多，因得大水之力枝条长长。
chapters.en: Therefore his height was exalted above all the trees of the field, and his boughs were multiplied, and his branches became long because of the multitude of waters, when he shot forth.

chapters.zh: 空中的飞鸟都在枝子上搭窝；田野的走兽都在枝条下生子；所有大国的人民都在他荫下居住。
chapters.en: All the fowls of heaven made their nests in his boughs, and under his branches did all the beasts of the field bring forth their young, and under his shadow dwelt all great nations.

chapters.zh: 树大条长，成为荣美，因为根在众水之旁。
chapters.en: Thus was he fair in his greatness, in the length of his branches: for his root was by great waters.

chapters.zh: 神园中的香柏树不能遮蔽他；松树不及他的枝子；枫树不及他的枝条；神园中的树都没有他荣美。
chapters.en: The cedars in the garden of God could not hide him: the fir trees were not like his boughs, and the chesnut trees were not like his branches; nor any tree in the garden of God was like unto him in his beauty.

chapters.zh: 我使他的枝条蕃多，成为荣美，以致神伊甸园中的树都嫉妒他。
chapters.en: I have made him fair by the multitude of his branches: so that all the trees of Eden, that in the garden of God, envied him.

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：因他高大，树尖插入云中，心骄气傲，
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Because thou hast lifted up thyself in height, and he hath shot up his top among the thick boughs, and his heart is lifted up in his height;

chapters.zh: 我就必将他交给列国中大有威势的人；他必定办他。我因他的罪恶，已经驱逐他。
chapters.en: I have therefore delivered him into the hand of the mighty one of the heathen; he shall surely deal with him: I have driven him out for his wickedness.

chapters.zh: 外邦人，就是列邦中强暴的，将他砍断弃掉。他的枝条落在山间和一切谷中，他的枝子折断，落在地的一切河旁。地上的众民已经走去，离开他的荫下。
chapters.en: And strangers, the terrible of the nations, have cut him off, and have left him: upon the mountains and in all the valleys his branches are fallen, and his boughs are broken by all the rivers of the land; and all the people of the earth are gone down from his shadow, and have left him.

chapters.zh: 空中的飞鸟都要宿在这败落的树上，田野的走兽都要卧在他的枝条下，
chapters.en: Upon his ruin shall all the fowls of the heaven remain, and all the beasts of the field shall be upon his branches:

chapters.zh: 好使水旁的诸树不因高大而自尊，也不将树尖插入云中，并且那些得水滋润、有势力的，也不得高大自立。因为他们在世人中，和下坑的人都被交与死亡，到阴府去了。
chapters.en: To the end that none of all the trees by the waters exalt themselves for their height, neither shoot up their top among the thick boughs, neither their trees stand up in their height, all that drink water: for they are all delivered unto death, to the nether parts of the earth, in the midst of the children of men, with them that go down to the pit.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：他下阴间的那日，我便使人悲哀。我为他遮盖深渊，使江河凝结，大水停流；我也使利巴嫩为他凄惨，田野的诸树都因他发昏。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; In the day when he went down to the grave I caused a mourning: I covered the deep for him, and I restrained the floods thereof, and the great waters were stayed: and I caused Lebanon to mourn for him, and all the trees of the field fainted for him.

chapters.zh: 我将他扔到阴间，与下坑的人一同下去。那时，列国听见他坠落的响声就都震动，并且伊甸的一切树 ─ 就是利巴嫩得水滋润、最佳最美的树 ─ 都在阴府受了安慰。
chapters.en: I made the nations to shake at the sound of his fall, when I cast him down to hell with them that descend into the pit: and all the trees of Eden, the choice and best of Lebanon, all that drink water, shall be comforted in the nether parts of the earth.

chapters.zh: 他们也与他同下阴间，到被杀的人那里。他们曾作他的膀臂，在列国中他的荫下居住。
chapters.en: They also went down into hell with him unto slain with the sword; and his arm, dwelt under his shadow in the midst of the heathen.

chapters.zh: 在这样荣耀威势上，在伊甸园诸树中，谁能与你相比呢？然而你要与伊甸的诸树一同下到阴府，在未受割礼的人中，与被杀的人一同躺卧。法老和他的群众乃是如此。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: To whom art thou thus like in glory and in greatness among the trees of Eden? yet shalt thou be brought down with the trees of Eden unto the nether parts of the earth: thou shalt lie in the midst of the uncircumcised with slain by the sword. This Pharaoh and all his multitude, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 十二年十二月初一日，耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the twelfth year, in the twelfth month, in the first of the month, the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要为埃及王法老作哀歌，说：从前你在列国中，如同少壮狮子；现在你却像海中的大鱼。你冲出江河，用爪搅动诸水，使江河浑浊。
chapters.en: Son of man, take up a lamentation for Pharaoh king of Egypt, and say unto him, Thou art like a young lion of the nations, and thou as a whale in the seas: and thou camest forth with thy rivers, and troubledst the waters with thy feet, and fouledst their rivers.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我必用多国的人民，将我的网撒在你身上，把你拉上来。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; I will therefore spread out my net over thee with a company of many people; and they shall bring thee up in my net.

chapters.zh: 我必将你丢在地上，抛在田野，使空中的飞鸟都落在你身上，使遍地的野兽吃你得饱。
chapters.en: Then will I leave thee upon the land, I will cast thee forth upon the open field, and will cause all the fowls of the heaven to remain upon thee, and I will fill the beasts of the whole earth with thee.

chapters.zh: 我必将你的肉丢在山间，用你高大的尸首填满山谷。
chapters.en: And I will lay thy flesh upon the mountains, and fill the valleys with thy height.

chapters.zh: 我又必用你的血浇灌你所游泳之地，漫过山顶；河道都必充满。
chapters.en: I will also water with thy blood the land wherein thou swimmest, to the mountains; and the rivers shall be full of thee.

chapters.zh: 我将你扑灭的时候，要把天遮蔽，使众星昏暗，以密云遮掩太阳，月亮也不放光。
chapters.en: And when I shall put thee out, I will cover the heaven, and make the stars thereof dark; I will cover the sun with a cloud, and the moon shall not give her light.

chapters.zh: 我必使天上的亮光都在你以上变为昏暗，使你的地上黑暗。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: All the bright lights of heaven will I make dark over thee, and set darkness upon thy land, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 我使你败亡的风声传到你所不认识的各国。那时，我必使多民的心因你愁烦。
chapters.en: I will also vex the hearts of many people, when I shall bring thy destruction among the nations, into the countries which thou hast not known.

chapters.zh: 我在许多国民和君王面前向你抡我的刀，国民就必因你惊奇，君王也必因你极其恐慌。在你仆倒的日子，他们各人为自己的性命时刻战兢。
chapters.en: Yea, I will make many people amazed at thee, and their kings shall be horribly afraid for thee, when I shall brandish my sword before them; and they shall tremble at moment, every man for his own life, in the day of thy fall.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：巴比伦王的刀必临到你。
chapters.en: For thus saith the Lord GOD; The sword of the king of Babylon shall come upon thee.

chapters.zh: 我必藉勇士的刀使你的众民仆倒；这勇士都是列国中强暴的。他们必使埃及的骄傲归于无有；埃及的众民必被灭绝。
chapters.en: By the swords of the mighty will I cause thy multitude to fall, the terrible of the nations, all of them: and they shall spoil the pomp of Egypt, and all the multitude thereof shall be destroyed.

chapters.zh: 我必从埃及多水旁除灭所有的走兽；人脚兽蹄必不再搅浑这水。
chapters.en: I will destroy also all the beasts thereof from beside the great waters; neither shall the foot of man trouble them any more, nor the hoofs of beasts trouble them.

chapters.zh: 那时，我必使埃及河澄清，江河像油缓流。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Then will I make their waters deep, and cause their rivers to run like oil, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 我使埃及地变为荒废凄凉；这地缺少从前所充满的，又击杀其中一切的居民。那时，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: When I shall make the land of Egypt desolate, and the country shall be destitute of that whereof it was full, when I shall smite all them that dwell therein, then shall they know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人必用这哀歌去哀哭，列国的女子为埃及和他的群众也必以此悲哀。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: This the lamentation wherewith they shall lament her: the daughters of the nations shall lament her: they shall lament for her, for Egypt, and for all her multitude, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 十二年十二月十五日，耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: It came to pass also in the twelfth year, in the fifteenth of the month, the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要为埃及群众哀号，又要将埃及和有名之国的女子，并下坑的人，一同扔到阴府去。
chapters.en: Son of man, wail for the multitude of Egypt, and cast them down, her, and the daughters of the famous nations, unto the nether parts of the earth, with them that go down into the pit.

chapters.zh: 你埃及的美丽胜过谁呢？你下去与未受割礼的人一同躺卧罢！
chapters.en: Whom dost thou pass in beauty? go down, and be thou laid with the uncircumcised.

chapters.zh: 他们必在被杀的人中仆到。他被交给刀剑，要把他和他的群众拉去。
chapters.en: They shall fall in the midst of slain by the sword: she is delivered to the sword: draw her and all her multitudes.

chapters.zh: 强盛的勇士要在阴间对埃及王和帮助他的说话；他们是未受割礼被杀的人，已经下去，躺卧不动。
chapters.en: The strong among the mighty shall speak to him out of the midst of hell with them that help him: they are gone down, they lie uncircumcised, slain by the sword.

chapters.zh: 亚述和他的众民都在那里，他民的坟墓在他四围；他们都是被杀倒在刀下的。
chapters.en: Asshur there and all her company: his graves about him: all of them slain, fallen by the sword:

chapters.zh: 他们的坟墓在坑中极深之处。他的众民在他坟墓的四围，都是被杀倒在刀下的；他们曾在活人之地使人惊恐。
chapters.en: Whose graves are set in the sides of the pit, and her company is round about her grave: all of them slain, fallen by the sword, which caused terror in the land of the living.

chapters.zh: 以拦也在那里，他的群众在他坟墓的四围，都是被杀倒在刀下、未受割礼而下阴府的；他们曾在活人之地使人惊恐，并且与下坑的人一同担当羞辱。
chapters.en: There Elam and all her multitude round about her grave, all of them slain, fallen by the sword, which are gone down uncircumcised into the nether parts of the earth, which caused their terror in the land of the living; yet have they borne their shame with them that go down to the pit.

chapters.zh: 人给他和他的群众在被杀的人中设立床榻。他民的坟墓在他四围，他们都是未受割礼被刀杀的；他们曾在活人之地使人惊恐，并且与下坑的人一同担当羞辱。以拦已经放在被杀的人中。
chapters.en: They have set her a bed in the midst of the slain with all her multitude: her graves round about him: all of them uncircumcised, slain by the sword: though their terror was caused in the land of the living, yet have they borne their shame with them that go down to the pit: he is put in the midst of slain.

chapters.zh: 米设、土巴，和他们的群众都在那里。他民的坟墓在他四围，他们都是未受割礼被刀杀的；他们曾在活人之地使人惊恐。
chapters.en: There Meshech, Tubal, and all her multitude: her graves round about him: all of them uncircumcised, slain by the sword, though they caused their terror in the land of the living.

chapters.zh: 他们不得与那未受割礼仆倒的勇士一同躺卧；这些勇士带著兵器下阴间，头枕刀剑，骨头上有本身的罪孽；他们曾在活人之地使勇士惊恐。
chapters.en: And they shall not lie with the mighty fallen of the uncircumcised, which are gone down to hell with their weapons of war: and they have laid their swords under their heads, but their iniquities shall be upon their bones, though the terror of the mighty in the land of the living.

chapters.zh: 法老啊，你必在未受割礼的人中败坏，与那些被杀的人一同躺卧。
chapters.en: Yea, thou shalt be broken in the midst of the uncircumcised, and shalt lie with slain with the sword.

chapters.zh: 以东也在那里。他君王和一切首领虽然仗著势力，还是放在被杀的人中；他们必与未受割礼的和下坑的人一同躺卧。
chapters.en: There Edom, her kings, and all her princes, which with their might are laid by slain by the sword: they shall lie with the uncircumcised, and with them that go down to the pit.

chapters.zh: 在那里有北方的众王子和一切西顿人，都与被杀的人下去。他们虽然仗著势力使人惊恐，还是蒙羞。他们未受割礼，和被刀杀的一同躺卧，与下坑的人一同担当羞辱。
chapters.en: There the princes of the north, all of them, and all the Zidonians, which are gone down with the slain; with their terror they are ashamed of their might; and they lie uncircumcised with slain by the sword, and bear their shame with them that go down to the pit.

chapters.zh: 法老看见他们，便为他被杀的军队受安慰。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Pharaoh shall see them, and shall be comforted over all his multitude, Pharaoh and all his army slain by the sword, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 我任凭法老在活人之地使人惊恐，法老和他的群众必放在未受割礼和被杀的人中。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: For I have caused my terror in the land of the living: and he shall be laid in the midst of the uncircumcised with slain with the sword, Pharaoh and all his multitude, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Again the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要告诉本国的子民说：我使刀剑临到哪一国，那一国的民从他们中间选立一人为守望的。
chapters.en: Son of man, speak to the children of thy people, and say unto them, When I bring the sword upon a land, if the people of the land take a man of their coasts, and set him for their watchman:

chapters.zh: 他见刀剑临到那地，若吹角警戒众民，
chapters.en: If when he seeth the sword come upon the land, he blow the trumpet, and warn the people;

chapters.zh: 凡听见角声不受警戒的，刀剑若来除灭了他，他的罪（原文是血）就必归到自己的头上。
chapters.en: Then whosoever heareth the sound of the trumpet, and taketh not warning; if the sword come, and take him away, his blood shall be upon his own head.

chapters.zh: 他听见角声，不受警戒，他的罪必归到自己的身上；他若受警戒，便是救了自己的性命。
chapters.en: He heard the sound of the trumpet, and took not warning; his blood shall be upon him. But he that taketh warning shall deliver his soul.

chapters.zh: 倘若守望的人见刀剑临到，不吹角，以致民不受警戒，刀剑来杀了他们中间的一个人，他虽然死在罪孽之中，我却要向守望的人讨他丧命的罪（原文是血）。
chapters.en: But if the watchman see the sword come, and blow not the trumpet, and the people be not warned; if the sword come, and take person from among them, he is taken away in his iniquity; but his blood will I require at the watchman's hand.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，我照样立你作以色列家守望的人。所以你要听我口中的话，替我警戒他们。
chapters.en: So thou, O son of man, I have set thee a watchman unto the house of Israel; therefore thou shalt hear the word at my mouth, and warn them from me.

chapters.zh: 我对恶人说：恶人哪，你必要死！你 ─ 以西结若不开口警戒恶人，使他离开所行的道，这恶人必死在罪孽之中，我却要向你讨他丧命的罪（原文是血）。
chapters.en: When I say unto the wicked, O wicked , thou shalt surely die; if thou dost not speak to warn the wicked from his way, that wicked shall die in his iniquity; but his blood will I require at thine hand.

chapters.zh: 倘若你警戒恶人转离所行的道，他仍不转离，他必死在罪孽之中，你却救自己脱离了罪。
chapters.en: Nevertheless, if thou warn the wicked of his way to turn from it; if he do not turn from his way, he shall die in his iniquity; but thou hast delivered thy soul.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要对以色列家说：你们常说：我们的过犯罪恶在我们身上，我们必因此消灭，怎能存活呢？
chapters.en: Therefore, O thou son of man, speak unto the house of Israel; Thus ye speak, saying, If our transgressions and our sins upon us, and we pine away in them, how should we then live?

chapters.zh: 你对他们说，主耶和华说：我指著我的永生起誓，我断不喜悦恶人死亡，惟喜悦恶人转离所行的道而活。以色列家啊，你们转回，转回罢！离开恶道，何必死亡呢？
chapters.en: Say unto them, I live, saith the Lord GOD, I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked; but that the wicked turn from his way and live: turn ye, turn ye from your evil ways; for why will ye die, O house of Israel?

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要对本国的人民说：义人的义，在犯罪之日不能救他；至于恶人的恶，在他转离恶行之日也不能使他倾倒；义人在犯罪之日也不能因他的义存活。
chapters.en: Therefore, thou son of man, say unto the children of thy people, The righteousness of the righteous shall not deliver him in the day of his transgression: as for the wickedness of the wicked, he shall not fall thereby in the day that he turneth from his wickedness; neither shall the righteous be able to live for his in the day that he sinneth.

chapters.zh: 我对义人说：你必定存活！他若倚靠他的义而作罪孽，他所行的义都不被记念。他必因所作的罪孽死亡。
chapters.en: When I shall say to the righteous, he shall surely live; if he trust to his own righteousness, and commit iniquity, all his righteousnesses shall not be remembered; but for his iniquity that he hath committed, he shall die for it.

chapters.zh: 再者，我对恶人说：你必定死亡！他若转离他的罪，行正直与合理的事：
chapters.en: Again, when I say unto the wicked, Thou shalt surely die; if he turn from his sin, and do that which is lawful and right;

chapters.zh: 还人的当头和所抢夺的，遵行生命的律例，不作罪孽，他必定存活，不致死亡。
chapters.en: the wicked restore the pledge, give again that he had robbed, walk in the statutes of life, without committing iniquity; he shall surely live, he shall not die.

chapters.zh: 他所犯的一切罪必不被记念。他行了正直与合理的事，必定存活。
chapters.en: None of his sins that he hath committed shall be mentioned unto him: he hath done that which is lawful and right; he shall surely live.

chapters.zh: 你本国的子民还说：主的道不公平。其实他们的道不公平。
chapters.en: Yet the children of thy people say, The way of the Lord is not equal: but as for them, their way is not equal.

chapters.zh: 义人转离他的义而作罪孽，就必因此死亡。
chapters.en: When the righteous turneth from his righteousness, and committeth iniquity, he shall even die thereby.

chapters.zh: 恶人转离他的恶，行正直与合理的事，就必因此存活。
chapters.en: But if the wicked turn from his wickedness, and do that which is lawful and right, he shall live thereby.

chapters.zh: 你们还说：主的道不公平。以色列家啊，我必按你们各人所行的审判你们。
chapters.en: Yet ye say, The way of the Lord is not equal. O ye house of Israel, I will judge you every one after his ways.

chapters.zh: 我们被掳之后十二年十月初五日，有人从耶路撒冷逃到我这里，说：城已攻破。
chapters.en: And it came to pass in the twelfth year of our captivity, in the tenth , in the fifth of the month, one that had escaped out of Jerusalem came unto me, saying, The city is smitten.

chapters.zh: 逃来的人未到前一日的晚上，耶和华的灵（原文是手）降在我身上，开我的口。到第二日早晨，那人来到我这里，我口就开了，不再缄默。
chapters.en: Now the hand of the LORD was upon me in the evening, afore he that was escaped came; and had opened my mouth, until he came to me in the morning; and my mouth was opened, and I was no more dumb.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Then the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，住在以色列荒废之地的人说：亚伯拉罕独自一人能得这地为业，我们人数众多，这地更是给我们为业的。
chapters.en: Son of man, they that inhabit those wastes of the land of Israel speak, saying, Abraham was one, and he inherited the land: but we many; the land is given us for inheritance.

chapters.zh: 所以你要对他们说，主耶和华如此说：你们吃带血的物，仰望偶像，并且杀人流血，你们还能得这地为业么？
chapters.en: Wherefore say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Ye eat with the blood, and lift up your eyes toward your idols, and shed blood: and shall ye possess the land?

chapters.zh: 你们倚仗自己的刀剑行可憎的事，人人玷污邻舍的妻，你们还能得这地为业么？
chapters.en: Ye stand upon your sword, ye work abomination, and ye defile every one his neighbour's wife: and shall ye possess the land?

chapters.zh: 你要对他们这样说，主耶和华如此说：我指著我的永生起誓，在荒场中的，必倒在刀下；在田野间的，必交给野兽吞吃；在保障和洞里的，必遭瘟疫而死。
chapters.en: Say thou thus unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; I live, surely they that in the wastes shall fall by the sword, and him that in the open field will I give to the beasts to be devoured, and they that in the forts and in the caves shall die of the pestilence.

chapters.zh: 我必使这地荒凉，令人惊骇；他因势力而有的骄傲也必止息。以色列的山都必荒凉，无人经过。
chapters.en: For I will lay the land most desolate, and the pomp of her strength shall cease; and the mountains of Israel shall be desolate, that none shall pass through.

chapters.zh: 我因他们所行一切可憎的事使地荒凉，令人惊骇。那时，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Then shall they know that I the LORD, when I have laid the land most desolate because of all their abominations which they have committed.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你本国的子民在墙垣旁边、在房屋门口谈论你。弟兄对弟兄彼此说：来罢！听听有甚么话从耶和华而出。
chapters.en: Also, thou son of man, the children of thy people still are talking against thee by the walls and in the doors of the houses, and speak one to another, every one to his brother, saying, Come, I pray you, and hear what is the word that cometh forth from the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他们来到你这里如同民来聚会，坐在你面前彷佛是我的民。他们听你的话却不去行；因为他们的口多显爱情，心却追随财利。
chapters.en: And they come unto thee as the people cometh, and they sit before thee my people, and they hear thy words, but they will not do them: for with their mouth they shew much love, their heart goeth after their covetousness.

chapters.zh: 他们看你如善于奏乐、声音幽雅之人所唱的雅歌，他们听你的话却不去行。
chapters.en: And, lo, thou unto them as a very lovely song of one that hath a pleasant voice, and can play well on an instrument: for they hear thy words, but they do them not.

chapters.zh: 看哪，所说的快要应验；应验了，他们就知道在他们中间有了先知。
chapters.en: And when this cometh to pass, (lo, it will come,) then shall they know that a prophet hath been among them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要向以色列的牧人发预言，攻击他们，说，主耶和华如此说：祸哉！以色列的牧人只知牧养自己。牧人岂不当牧养群羊么？
chapters.en: Son of man, prophesy against the shepherds of Israel, prophesy, and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD unto the shepherds; Woe to the shepherds of Israel that do feed themselves! should not the shepherds feed the flocks?

chapters.zh: 你们吃脂油、穿羊毛、宰肥壮的，却不牧养群羊。
chapters.en: Ye eat the fat, and ye clothe you with the wool, ye kill them that are fed: ye feed not the flock.

chapters.zh: 瘦弱的，你们没有养壮；有病的，你们没有医治；受伤的，你们没有缠裹；被逐的，你们没有领回；失丧的，你们没有寻找；但用强暴严严地辖制。
chapters.en: The diseased have ye not strengthened, neither have ye healed that which was sick, neither have ye bound up broken, neither have ye brought again that which was driven away, neither have ye sought that which was lost; but with force and with cruelty have ye ruled them.

chapters.zh: 因无牧人，羊就分散；既分散，便作了一切野兽的食物。
chapters.en: And they were scattered, because no shepherd: and they became meat to all the beasts of the field, when they were scattered.

chapters.zh: 我的羊在诸山间、在各高冈上流离，在全地上分散，无人去寻，无人去找。
chapters.en: My sheep wandered through all the mountains, and upon every high hill: yea, my flock was scattered upon all the face of the earth, and none did search or seek .

chapters.zh: 所以，你们这些牧人要听耶和华的话。
chapters.en: Therefore, ye shepherds, hear the word of the LORD;

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：我指著我的永生起誓，我的羊因无牧人就成为掠物，也作了一切野兽的食物。我的牧人不寻找我的羊；这些牧人只知牧养自己，并不牧养我的羊。
chapters.en: I live, saith the Lord GOD, surely because my flock became a prey, and my flock became meat to every beast of the field, because no shepherd, neither did my shepherds search for my flock, but the shepherds fed themselves, and fed not my flock;

chapters.zh: 所以你们这些牧人要听耶和华的话。
chapters.en: Therefore, O ye shepherds, hear the word of the LORD;

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我必与牧人为敌，必向他们的手追讨我的羊，使他们不再牧放群羊；牧人也不再牧养自己。我必救我的羊脱离他们的口，不再作他们的食物。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I against the shepherds; and I will require my flock at their hand, and cause them to cease from feeding the flock; neither shall the shepherds feed themselves any more; for I will deliver my flock from their mouth, that they may not be meat for them.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：看哪，我必亲自寻找我的羊，将他们寻见。
chapters.en: For thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I, I, will both search my sheep, and seek them out.

chapters.zh: 牧人在羊群四散的日子怎样寻找他的羊，我必照样寻找我的羊。这些羊在密云黑暗的日子散到各处，我必从那里救回他们来。
chapters.en: As a shepherd seeketh out his flock in the day that he is among his sheep scattered; so will I seek out my sheep, and will deliver them out of all places where they have been scattered in the cloudy and dark day.

chapters.zh: 我必从万民中领出他们，从各国内聚集他们，引导他们归回故土，也必在以色列山上 ─ 一切溪水旁边、境内一切可居之处 ─ 牧养他们。
chapters.en: And I will bring them out from the people, and gather them from the countries, and will bring them to their own land, and feed them upon the mountains of Israel by the rivers, and in all the inhabited places of the country.

chapters.zh: 我必在美好的草场牧养他们。他们的圈必在以色列高处的山上，他们必在佳美之圈中躺卧，也在以色列山肥美的草场吃草。
chapters.en: I will feed them in a good pasture, and upon the high mountains of Israel shall their fold be: there shall they lie in a good fold, and a fat pasture shall they feed upon the mountains of Israel.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：我必亲自作我羊的牧人，使他们得以躺卧。
chapters.en: I will feed my flock, and I will cause them to lie down, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 失丧的，我必寻找；被逐的，我必领回；受伤的，我必缠裹；有病的，我必医治；只是肥的壮的，我必除灭，也要秉公牧养他们。
chapters.en: I will seek that which was lost, and bring again that which was driven away, and will bind up broken, and will strengthen that which was sick: but I will destroy the fat and the strong; I will feed them with judgment.

chapters.zh: 我的羊群哪，论到你们，主耶和华如此说：我必在羊与羊中间、公绵羊与公山羊中间施行判断。
chapters.en: And you, O my flock, thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I judge between cattle and cattle, between the rams and the he goats.

chapters.zh: 你们这些肥壮的羊，在美好的草场吃草还以为小事么？剩下的草，你们竟用蹄践踏了；你们喝清水，剩下的水，你们竟用蹄搅浑了。
chapters.en: a small thing unto you to have eaten up the good pasture, but ye must tread down with your feet the residue of your pastures? and to have drunk of the deep waters, but ye must foul the residue with your feet?

chapters.zh: 至于我的羊，只得吃你们所践踏的，喝你们所搅浑的。
chapters.en: And my flock, they eat that which ye have trodden with your feet; and they drink that which ye have fouled with your feet.

chapters.zh: 所以，主耶和华如此说：我必在肥羊和瘦羊中间施行判断。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD unto them; Behold, I, I, will judge between the fat cattle and between the lean cattle.

chapters.zh: 因为你们用胁用肩拥挤一切瘦弱的，又用角牴触，以致使他们四散。
chapters.en: Because ye have thrust with side and with shoulder, and pushed all the diseased with your horns, till ye have scattered them abroad;

chapters.zh: 所以，我必拯救我的群羊不再作掠物；我也必在羊和羊中间施行判断。
chapters.en: Therefore will I save my flock, and they shall no more be a prey; and I will judge between cattle and cattle.

chapters.zh: 我必立一牧人照管他们，牧养他们，就是我的仆人大卫。他必牧养他们，作他们的牧人。
chapters.en: And I will set up one shepherd over them, and he shall feed them, my servant David; he shall feed them, and he shall be their shepherd.

chapters.zh: 我 ─ 耶和华必作他们的神，我的仆人大卫必在他们中间作王。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And I the LORD will be their God, and my servant David a prince among them; I the LORD have spoken .

chapters.zh: 我必与他们立平安的约，使恶兽从境内断绝，他们就必安居在旷野，躺卧在林中。
chapters.en: And I will make with them a covenant of peace, and will cause the evil beasts to cease out of the land: and they shall dwell safely in the wilderness, and sleep in the woods.

chapters.zh: 我必使他们与我山的四围成为福源，我也必叫时雨落下，必有福如甘霖而降。
chapters.en: And I will make them and the places round about my hill a blessing; and I will cause the shower to come down in his season; there shall be showers of blessing.

chapters.zh: 田野的树必结果，地也必有出产；他们必在故土安然居住。我折断他们所负的轭，救他们脱离那以他们为奴之人的手；那时，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And the tree of the field shall yield her fruit, and the earth shall yield her increase, and they shall be safe in their land, and shall know that I the LORD, when I have broken the bands of their yoke, and delivered them out of the hand of those that served themselves of them.

chapters.zh: 他们必不再作外邦人的掠物，地上的野兽也不再吞吃他们；却要安然居住，无人惊吓。
chapters.en: And they shall no more be a prey to the heathen, neither shall the beast of the land devour them; but they shall dwell safely, and none shall make afraid.

chapters.zh: 我必给他们兴起有名的植物；他们在境内不再为饥荒所灭，也不再受外邦人的羞辱，
chapters.en: And I will raise up for them a plant of renown, and they shall be no more consumed with hunger in the land, neither bear the shame of the heathen any more.

chapters.zh: 必知道我、耶和华 ─ 他们的神是与他们同在的，并知道他们 ─ 以色列家是我的民。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Thus shall they know that I the LORD their God with them, and they, the house of Israel, my people, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 你们作我的羊，我草场上的羊，乃是以色列人，我也是你们的神。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And ye my flock, the flock of my pasture, men, I your God, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: Moreover the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要面向西珥山发预言，攻击他，
chapters.en: Son of man, set thy face against mount Seir, and prophesy against it,

chapters.zh: 对他说，主耶和华如此说：西珥山哪，我与你为敌，必向你伸手攻击你，使你荒凉，令人惊骇。
chapters.en: And say unto it, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, O mount Seir, I against thee, and I will stretch out mine hand against thee, and I will make thee most desolate.

chapters.zh: 我必使你的城邑变为荒场，成为凄凉。你就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: I will lay thy cities waste, and thou shalt be desolate, and thou shalt know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 因为你永怀仇恨，在以色列人遭灾、罪孽到了尽头的时候，将他们交与刀剑，
chapters.en: Because thou hast had a perpetual hatred, and hast shed the children of Israel by the force of the sword in the time of their calamity, in the time iniquity an end:

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华说：我指著我的永生起誓，我必使你遭遇流血的报应，罪（原文是血；本节同）必追赶你；你既不恨恶杀人流血，所以这罪必追赶你。
chapters.en: Therefore, I live, saith the Lord GOD, I will prepare thee unto blood, and blood shall pursue thee: sith thou hast not hated blood, even blood shall pursue thee.

chapters.zh: 我必使西珥山荒凉，令人惊骇，来往经过的人我必剪除。
chapters.en: Thus will I make mount Seir most desolate, and cut off from it him that passeth out and him that returneth.

chapters.zh: 我必使西珥山满有被杀的人。被刀杀的，必倒在你小山和山谷，并一切的溪水中。
chapters.en: And I will fill his mountains with his slain : in thy hills, and in thy valleys, and in all thy rivers, shall they fall that are slain with the sword.

chapters.zh: 我必使你永远荒凉，使你的城邑无人居住，你的民就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: I will make thee perpetual desolations, and thy cities shall not return: and ye shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 因为你曾说：这二国这二邦必归于我，我必得为业（其实耶和华仍在那里），
chapters.en: Because thou hast said, These two nations and these two countries shall be mine, and we will possess it; whereas the LORD was there:

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华说：我指著我的永生起誓，我必照你的怒气和你从仇恨中向他们所发的嫉妒待你。我审判你的时候，必将自己显明在他们中间。
chapters.en: Therefore, I live, saith the Lord GOD, I will even do according to thine anger, and according to thine envy which thou hast used out of thy hatred against them; and I will make myself known among them, when I have judged thee.

chapters.zh: 你也必知道我 ─ 耶和华听见了你的一切毁谤，就是你攻击以色列山的话，说：这些山荒凉，是归我们吞灭的。
chapters.en: And thou shalt know that I the LORD, I have heard all thy blasphemies which thou hast spoken against the mountains of Israel, saying, They are laid desolate, they are given us to consume.

chapters.zh: 你们也用口向我夸大，增添与我反对的话，我都听见了。
chapters.en: Thus with your mouth ye have boasted against me, and have multiplied your words against me: I have heard .

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：全地欢乐的时候，我必使你荒凉。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; When the whole earth rejoiceth, I will make thee desolate.

chapters.zh: 你怎样因以色列家的地业荒凉而喜乐，我必照你所行的待你。西珥山哪，你和以东全地必都荒凉。你们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: As thou didst rejoice at the inheritance of the house of Israel, because it was desolate, so will I do unto thee: thou shalt be desolate, O mount Seir, and all Idumea, all of it: and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要对以色列山发预言说：以色列山哪，要听耶和华的话。
chapters.en: Also, thou son of man, prophesy unto the mountains of Israel, and say, Ye mountains of Israel, hear the word of the LORD:

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：因仇敌说：阿哈！这永久的山冈都归我们为业了！
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; Because the enemy hath said against you, Aha, even the ancient high places are ours in possession:

chapters.zh: 所以要发预言说，主耶和华如此说：因为敌人使你荒凉，四围吞吃，好叫你归与其余的外邦人为业，并且多嘴多舌的人提起你来，百姓也说你有臭名。
chapters.en: Therefore prophesy and say, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Because they have made desolate, and swallowed you up on every side, that ye might be a possession unto the residue of the heathen, and ye are taken up in the lips of talkers, and an infamy of the people:

chapters.zh: 故此，以色列山要听主耶和华的话。大山小冈、水沟山谷、荒废之地、被弃之城，为四围其余的外邦人所占据、所讥刺的，
chapters.en: Therefore, ye mountains of Israel, hear the word of the Lord GOD; Thus saith the Lord GOD to the mountains, and to the hills, to the rivers, and to the valleys, to the desolate wastes, and to the cities that are forsaken, which became a prey and derision to the residue of the heathen that round about;

chapters.zh: 主耶和华对你们如此说：我真发愤恨如火，责备那其余的外邦人和以东的众人。他们快乐满怀，心存恨恶，将我的地归自己为业，又看为被弃的掠物。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Surely in the fire of my jealousy have I spoken against the residue of the heathen, and against all Idumea, which have appointed my land into their possession with the joy of all heart, with despiteful minds, to cast it out for a prey.

chapters.zh: 所以，你要指著以色列地说预言，对大山小冈、水沟山谷说，主耶和华如此说：我发愤恨和忿怒说，因你们曾受外邦人的羞辱，
chapters.en: Prophesy therefore concerning the land of Israel, and say unto the mountains, and to the hills, to the rivers, and to the valleys, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I have spoken in my jealousy and in my fury, because ye have borne the shame of the heathen:

chapters.zh: 所以我起誓说：你们四围的外邦人总要担当自己的羞辱。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; I have lifted up mine hand, Surely the heathen that about you, they shall bear their shame.

chapters.zh: 以色列山哪，你必发枝条，为我的民以色列结果子，因为他们快要来到。
chapters.en: But ye, O mountains of Israel, ye shall shoot forth your branches, and yield your fruit to my people of Israel; for they are at hand to come.

chapters.zh: 看哪，我是帮助你的，也必向你转意，使你得以耕种。
chapters.en: For, behold, I for you, and I will turn unto you, and ye shall be tilled and sown:

chapters.zh: 我必使以色列全家的人数在你上面增多，城邑有人居住，荒场再被建造。
chapters.en: And I will multiply men upon you, all the house of Israel, all of it: and the cities shall be inhabited, and the wastes shall be builded:

chapters.zh: 我必使人和牲畜在你上面加增；他们必生养众多。我要使你照旧有人居住，并要赐福与你比先前更多，你就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And I will multiply upon you man and beast; and they shall increase and bring fruit: and I will settle you after your old estates, and will do better than at your beginnings: and ye shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 我必使人，就是我的民以色列，行在你上面。他们必得你为业；你也不再使他们丧子。
chapters.en: Yea, I will cause men to walk upon you, my people Israel; and they shall possess thee, and thou shalt be their inheritance, and thou shalt no more henceforth bereave them .

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：因为人对你说：你是吞吃人的，又使国民丧子，
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; Because they say unto you, Thou devourest up men, and hast bereaved thy nations;

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华说：你必不再吞吃人，也不再使国民丧子。
chapters.en: Therefore thou shalt devour men no more, neither bereave thy nations any more, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 我使你不再听见各国的羞辱，不再受万民的辱骂，也不再使国民绊跌。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Neither will I cause to hear in thee the shame of the heathen any more, neither shalt thou bear the reproach of the people any more, neither shalt thou cause thy nations to fall any more, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: Moreover the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，以色列家住在本地的时候，在行动作为上玷污那地。他们的行为在我面前，好像正在经期的妇人那样污秽。
chapters.en: Son of man, when the house of Israel dwelt in their own land, they defiled it by their own way and by their doings: their way was before me as the uncleanness of a removed woman.

chapters.zh: 所以我因他们在那地上流人的血，又因他们以偶像玷污那地，就把我的忿怒倾在他们身上。
chapters.en: Wherefore I poured my fury upon them for the blood that they had shed upon the land, and for their idols they had polluted it:

chapters.zh: 我将他们分散在列国，四散在列邦，按他们的行动作为惩罚他们。
chapters.en: And I scattered them among the heathen, and they were dispersed through the countries: according to their way and according to their doings I judged them.

chapters.zh: 他们到了所去的列国，就使我的圣名被亵渎；因为人谈论他们说，这是耶和华的民，是从耶和华的地出来的。
chapters.en: And when they entered unto the heathen, whither they went, they profaned my holy name, when they said to them, These the people of the LORD, and are gone forth out of his land.

chapters.zh: 我却顾惜我的圣名，就是以色列家在所到的列国中所亵渎的。
chapters.en: But I had pity for mine holy name, which the house of Israel had profaned among the heathen, whither they went.

chapters.zh: 所以，你要对以色列家说，主耶和华如此说：以色列家啊，我行这事不是为你们，乃是为我的圣名，就是在你们到的列国中所亵渎的。
chapters.en: Therefore say unto the house of Israel, Thus saith the Lord GOD; I do not for your sakes, O house of Israel, but for mine holy name's sake, which ye have profaned among the heathen, whither ye went.

chapters.zh: 我要使我的大名显为圣；这名在列国中已被亵渎，就是你们在他们中间所亵渎的。我在他们眼前，在你们身上显为圣的时候，他们就知道我是耶和华。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And I will sanctify my great name, which was profaned among the heathen, which ye have profaned in the midst of them; and the heathen shall know that I the LORD, saith the Lord GOD, when I shall be sanctified in you before their eyes.

chapters.zh: 我必从各国收取你们，从列邦聚集你们，引导你们归回本地。
chapters.en: For I will take you from among the heathen, and gather you out of all countries, and will bring you into your own land.

chapters.zh: 我必用清水洒在你们身上，你们就洁净了。我要洁净你们，使你们脱离一切的污秽，弃掉一切的偶像。
chapters.en: Then will I sprinkle clean water upon you, and ye shall be clean: from all your filthiness, and from all your idols, will I cleanse you.

chapters.zh: 我也要赐给你们一个新心，将新灵放在你们里面，又从你们的肉体中除掉石心，赐给你们肉心。
chapters.en: A new heart also will I give you, and a new spirit will I put within you: and I will take away the stony heart out of your flesh, and I will give you an heart of flesh.

chapters.zh: 我必将我的灵放在你们里面，使你们顺从我的律例，谨守遵行我的典章。
chapters.en: And I will put my spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes, and ye shall keep my judgments, and do .

chapters.zh: 你们必住在我所赐给你们列祖之地。你们要作我的子民，我要作你们的神。
chapters.en: And ye shall dwell in the land that I gave to your fathers; and ye shall be my people, and I will be your God.

chapters.zh: 我必救你们脱离一切的污秽，也必命五谷丰登，不使你们遭遇饥荒。
chapters.en: I will also save you from all your uncleannesses: and I will call for the corn, and will increase it, and lay no famine upon you.

chapters.zh: 我必使树木多结果子，田地多出土产，好叫你们不再因饥荒受外邦人的讥诮。
chapters.en: And I will multiply the fruit of the tree, and the increase of the field, that ye shall receive no more reproach of famine among the heathen.

chapters.zh: 那时，你们必追想你们的恶行和你们不善的作为，就因你们的罪孽和可憎的事厌恶自己。
chapters.en: Then shall ye remember your own evil ways, and your doings that not good, and shall lothe yourselves in your own sight for your iniquities and for your abominations.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：你们要知道，我这样行不是为你们。以色列家啊，当为自己的行为抱愧蒙羞。
chapters.en: Not for your sakes do I , saith the Lord GOD, be it known unto you: be ashamed and confounded for your own ways, O house of Israel.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我洁净你们，使你们脱离一切罪孽的日子，必使城邑有人居住，荒场再被建造。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; In the day that I shall have cleansed you from all your iniquities I will also cause to dwell in the cities, and the wastes shall be builded.

chapters.zh: 过路的人虽看为荒废之地，现今这荒废之地仍得耕种。
chapters.en: And the desolate land shall be tilled, whereas it lay desolate in the sight of all that passed by.

chapters.zh: 他们必说：这先前为荒废之地，现在成如伊甸园；这荒废凄凉、毁坏的城邑现在坚固有人居住。
chapters.en: And they shall say, This land that was desolate is become like the garden of Eden; and the waste and desolate and ruined cities fenced, are inhabited.

chapters.zh: 那时，在你们四围其余的外邦人必知道我 ─ 耶和华修造那毁坏之处，培植那荒废之地。我 ─ 耶和华说过，也必成就。
chapters.en: Then the heathen that are left round about you shall know that I the LORD build the ruined plant that that was desolate: I the LORD have spoken , and I will do .

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我要加增以色列家的人数，多如羊群。他们必为这事向我求问，我要给他们成就。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; I will yet this be enquired of by the house of Israel, to do for them; I will increase them with men like a flock.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷在守节作祭物所献的羊群怎样多，照样，荒凉的城邑必被人群充满。他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: As the holy flock, as the flock of Jerusalem in her solemn feasts; so shall the waste cities be filled with flocks of men: and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的灵（原文是手）降在我身上。耶和华藉他的灵带我出去，将我放在平原中；这平原遍满骸骨。
chapters.en: The hand of the LORD was upon me, and carried me out in the spirit of the LORD, and set me down in the midst of the valley which full of bones,

chapters.zh: 他使我从骸骨的四围经过，谁知在平原的骸骨甚多，而且极其枯干。
chapters.en: And caused me to pass by them round about: and, behold, very many in the open valley; and, lo, very dry.

chapters.zh: 他对我说：人子啊，这些骸骨能复活么？我说：主耶和华啊，你是知道的。
chapters.en: And he said unto me, Son of man, can these bones live? And I answered, O Lord GOD, thou knowest.

chapters.zh: 他又对我说：你向这些骸骨发预言说：枯干的骸骨啊，要听耶和华的话。
chapters.en: Again he said unto me, Prophesy upon these bones, and say unto them, O ye dry bones, hear the word of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华对这些骸骨如此说：我必使气息进入你们里面，你们就要活了。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD unto these bones; Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and ye shall live:

chapters.zh: 我必给你们加上筋，使你们长肉，又将皮遮蔽你们，使气息进入你们里面，你们就要活了；你们便知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And I will lay sinews upon you, and will bring up flesh upon you, and cover you with skin, and put breath in you, and ye shall live; and ye shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 于是，我遵命说预言。正说预言的时候，不料，有响声，有地震；骨与骨互相联络。
chapters.en: So I prophesied as I was commanded: and as I prophesied, there was a noise, and behold a shaking, and the bones came together, bone to his bone.

chapters.zh: 我观看，见骸骨上有筋，也长了肉，又有皮遮蔽其上，只是还没有气息。
chapters.en: And when I beheld, lo, the sinews and the flesh came up upon them, and the skin covered them above: but no breath in them.

chapters.zh: 主对我说：人子啊，你要发预言，向风发预言，说主耶和华如此说：气息啊，要从四方（原文是风）而来，吹在这些被杀的人身上，使他们活了。
chapters.en: Then said he unto me, Prophesy unto the wind, prophesy, son of man, and say to the wind, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Come from the four winds, O breath, and breathe upon these slain, that they may live.

chapters.zh: 于是我遵命说预言，气息就进入骸骨，骸骨便活了，并且站起来，成为极大的军队。
chapters.en: So I prophesied as he commanded me, and the breath came into them, and they lived, and stood up upon their feet, an exceeding great army.

chapters.zh: 主对我说：人子啊，这些骸骨就是以色列全家。他们说：我们的骨头枯干了，我们的指望失去了，我们灭绝净尽了。
chapters.en: Then he said unto me, Son of man, these bones are the whole house of Israel: behold, they say, Our bones are dried, and our hope is lost: we are cut off for our parts.

chapters.zh: 所以你要发预言对他们说，主耶和华如此说：我的民哪，我必开你们的坟墓，使你们从坟墓中出来，领你们进入以色列地。
chapters.en: Therefore prophesy and say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, O my people, I will open your graves, and cause you to come up out of your graves, and bring you into the land of Israel.

chapters.zh: 我的民哪，我开你们的坟墓，使你们从坟墓中出来，你们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And ye shall know that I the LORD, when I have opened your graves, O my people, and brought you up out of your graves,

chapters.zh: 我必将我的灵放在你们里面，你们就要活了。我将你们安置在本地，你们就知道我 ─ 耶和华如此说，也如此成就了。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And shall put my spirit in you, and ye shall live, and I shall place you in your own land: then shall ye know that I the LORD have spoken , and performed , saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：
chapters.en: The word of the LORD came again unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要取一根木杖，在其上写为犹大和他的同伴以色列人；又取一根木杖，在其上写为约瑟，就是为以法莲，又为他的同伴以色列全家。
chapters.en: Moreover, thou son of man, take thee one stick, and write upon it, For Judah, and for the children of Israel his companions: then take another stick, and write upon it, For Joseph, the stick of Ephraim, and all the house of Israel his companions:

chapters.zh: 你要使这两根木杖接连为一，在你手中成为一根。
chapters.en: And join them one to another into one stick; and they shall become one in thine hand.

chapters.zh: 你本国的子民问你说：这是甚么意思？你不指示我们么？
chapters.en: And when the children of thy people shall speak unto thee, saying, Wilt thou not shew us what thou by these?

chapters.zh: 你就对他们说：主耶和华如此说：我要将约瑟和他同伴以色列支派的杖，就是那在以法莲手中的，与犹大的杖一同接连为一，在我手中成为一根。
chapters.en: Say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will take the stick of Joseph, which in the hand of Ephraim, and the tribes of Israel his fellows, and will put them with him, with the stick of Judah, and make them one stick, and they shall be one in mine hand.

chapters.zh: 你所写的那两根杖要在他们眼前拿在手中，
chapters.en: And the sticks whereon thou writest shall be in thine hand before their eyes.

chapters.zh: 要对他们说，主耶和华如此说：我要将以色列人从他们所到的各国收取，又从四围聚集他们，引导他们归回本地。
chapters.en: And say unto them, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I will take the children of Israel from among the heathen, whither they be gone, and will gather them on every side, and bring them into their own land:

chapters.zh: 我要使他们在那地，在以色列山上成为一国，有一王作他们众民的王。他们不再为二国，决不再分为二国；
chapters.en: And I will make them one nation in the land upon the mountains of Israel; and one king shall be king to them all: and they shall be no more two nations, neither shall they be divided into two kingdoms any more at all:

chapters.zh: 也不再因偶像和可憎的物，并一切的罪过玷污自己。我却要救他们出离一切的住处，就是他们犯罪的地方；我要洁净他们，如此，他们要作我的子民，我要作他们的神。
chapters.en: Neither shall they defile themselves any more with their idols, nor with their detestable things, nor with any of their transgressions: but I will save them out of all their dwellingplaces, wherein they have sinned, and will cleanse them: so shall they be my people, and I will be their God.

chapters.zh: 我的仆人大卫必作他们的王；众民必归一个牧人。他们必顺从我的典章，谨守遵行我的律例。
chapters.en: And David my servant king over them; and they all shall have one shepherd: they shall also walk in my judgments, and observe my statutes, and do them.

chapters.zh: 他们必住在我赐给我仆人雅各的地上，就是你们列祖所住之地。他们和他们的子孙，并子孙的子孙，都永远住在那里。我的仆人大卫必作他们的王，直到永远。
chapters.en: And they shall dwell in the land that I have given unto Jacob my servant, wherein your fathers have dwelt; and they shall dwell therein, they, and their children, and their children's children for ever: and my servant David their prince for ever.

chapters.zh: 并且我要与他们立平安的约，作为永约。我也要将他们安置在本地，使他们的人数增多，又在他们中间设立我的圣所，直到永远。
chapters.en: Moreover I will make a covenant of peace with them; it shall be an everlasting covenant with them: and I will place them, and multiply them, and will set my sanctuary in the midst of them for evermore.

chapters.zh: 我的居所必在他们中间；我要作他们的神，他们要作我的子民。
chapters.en: My tabernacle also shall be with them: yea, I will be their God, and they shall be my people.

chapters.zh: 我的圣所在以色列人中间直到永远，外邦人就必知道我是叫以色列成为圣的耶和华。
chapters.en: And the heathen shall know that I the LORD do sanctify Israel, when my sanctuary shall be in the midst of them for evermore.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: And the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要面向玛各地的歌革，就是罗施、米设、土巴的王发预言攻击他，
chapters.en: Son of man, set thy face against Gog, the land of Magog, the chief prince of Meshech and Tubal, and prophesy against him,

chapters.zh: 说主耶和华如此说：罗施、米设、土巴的王歌革啊，我与你为敌。
chapters.en: And say, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I against thee, O Gog, the chief prince of Meshech and Tubal:

chapters.zh: 我必用钩子钩住你的腮颊，调转你，将你和你的军兵、马匹、马兵带出来，都披挂整齐，成了大队，有大小盾牌，各拿刀剑。
chapters.en: And I will turn thee back, and put hooks into thy jaws, and I will bring thee forth, and all thine army, horses and horsemen, all of them clothed with all sorts a great company bucklers and shields, all of them handling swords:

chapters.zh: 波斯人、古实人，和弗人（又作吕彼亚人），各拿盾牌，头上戴盔；
chapters.en: Persia, Ethiopia, and Libya with them; all of them with shield and helmet:

chapters.zh: 歌篾人和他的军队，北方极处的陀迦玛族和他的军队，这许多国的民都同著你。
chapters.en: Gomer, and all his bands; the house of Togarmah of the north quarters, and all his bands: many people with thee.

chapters.zh: 那聚集到你这里的各队都当准备；你自己也要准备，作他们的大帅。
chapters.en: Be thou prepared, and prepare for thyself, thou, and all thy company that are assembled unto thee, and be thou a guard unto them.

chapters.zh: 过了多日，你必被差派。到末后之年，你必来到脱离刀剑从列国收回之地，到以色列常久荒凉的山上；但那从列国中招聚出来的必在其上安然居住。
chapters.en: After many days thou shalt be visited: in the latter years thou shalt come into the land brought back from the sword, gathered out of many people, against the mountains of Israel, which have been always waste: but it is brought forth out of the nations, and they shall dwell safely all of them.

chapters.zh: 你和你的军队，并同著你许多国的民，必如暴风上来，如密云遮盖地面。
chapters.en: Thou shalt ascend and come like a storm, thou shalt be like a cloud to cover the land, thou, and all thy bands, and many people with thee.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：到那时，你心必起意念，图谋恶计，
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; It shall also come to pass, at the same time shall things come into thy mind, and thou shalt think an evil thought:

chapters.zh: 说：我要上那无城墙的乡村，我要到那安静的民那里，他们都没有城墙，无门、无闩，安然居住。
chapters.en: And thou shalt say, I will go up to the land of unwalled villages; I will go to them that are at rest, that dwell safely, all of them dwelling without walls, and having neither bars nor gates,

chapters.zh: 我去要抢财为掳物，夺货为掠物，反手攻击那从前荒凉、现在有人居住之地，又攻击那住世界中间、从列国招聚、得了牲畜财货的民。
chapters.en: To take a spoil, and to take a prey; to turn thine hand upon the desolate places inhabited, and upon the people gathered out of the nations, which have gotten cattle and goods, that dwell in the midst of the land.

chapters.zh: 示巴人、底但人、他施的客商，和其间的少壮狮子都必问你说：你来要抢财为掳物么？你聚集军队要夺货为掠物么？要夺取金银，掳去牲畜、财货么？要抢夺许多财宝为掳物么？
chapters.en: Sheba, and Dedan, and the merchants of Tarshish, with all the young lions thereof, shall say unto thee, Art thou come to take a spoil? hast thou gathered thy company to take a prey? to carry away silver and gold, to take away cattle and goods, to take a great spoil?

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要因此发预言，对歌革说，主耶和华如此说：到我民以色列安然居住之日，你岂不知道么？
chapters.en: Therefore, son of man, prophesy and say unto Gog, Thus saith the Lord GOD; In that day when my people of Israel dwelleth safely, shalt thou not know ?

chapters.zh: 你必从本地，从北方的极处率领许多国的民来，都骑著马，乃一大队极多的军兵。
chapters.en: And thou shalt come from thy place out of the north parts, thou, and many people with thee, all of them riding upon horses, a great company, and a mighty army:

chapters.zh: 歌革啊，你必上来攻击我的民以色列，如密云遮盖地面。末后的日子，我必带你来攻击我的地，到我在外邦人眼前，在你身上显为圣的时候，好叫他们认识我。
chapters.en: And thou shalt come up against my people of Israel, as a cloud to cover the land; it shall be in the latter days, and I will bring thee against my land, that the heathen may know me, when I shall be sanctified in thee, O Gog, before their eyes.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我在古时藉我的仆人以色列的先知所说的，就是你么？当日他们多年预言我必带你来攻击以色列人。
chapters.en: Thus saith the Lord GOD; thou he of whom I have spoken in old time by my servants the prophets of Israel, which prophesied in those days years that I would bring thee against them?

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：歌革上来攻击以色列地的时候，我的怒气要从鼻孔里发出。
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass at the same time when Gog shall come against the land of Israel, saith the Lord GOD, my fury shall come up in my face.

chapters.zh: 我发愤恨和烈怒如火说：那日在以色列地必有大震动，
chapters.en: For in my jealousy in the fire of my wrath have I spoken, Surely in that day there shall be a great shaking in the land of Israel;

chapters.zh: 甚至海中的鱼、天空的鸟、田野的兽，并地上的一切昆虫，和其上的众人，因见我的面就都震动；山岭必崩裂，陡岩必塌陷，墙垣都必坍倒。
chapters.en: So that the fishes of the sea, and the fowls of the heaven, and the beasts of the field, and all creeping things that creep upon the earth, and all the men that upon the face of the earth, shall shake at my presence, and the mountains shall be thrown down, and the steep places shall fall, and every wall shall fall to the ground.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：我必命我的诸山发刀剑来攻击歌革；人都要用刀剑杀害弟兄。
chapters.en: And I will call for a sword against him throughout all my mountains, saith the Lord GOD: every man's sword shall be against his brother.

chapters.zh: 我必用瘟疫和流血的事刑罚他。我也必将暴雨、大雹与火，并硫磺降与他和他的军队，并他所率领的众民。
chapters.en: And I will plead against him with pestilence and with blood; and I will rain upon him, and upon his bands, and upon the many people that with him, an overflowing rain, and great hailstones, fire, and brimstone.

chapters.zh: 我必显为大，显为圣，在多国人的眼前显现；他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: Thus will I magnify myself, and sanctify myself; and I will be known in the eyes of many nations, and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，你要向歌革发预言攻击他，说主耶和华如此说：罗施、米设、土巴的王歌革啊，我与你为敌。
chapters.en: Therefore, thou son of man, prophesy against Gog, and say, Thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, I against thee, O Gog, the chief prince of Meshech and Tubal:

chapters.zh: 我必调转你，领你前往，使你从北方的极处上来，带你到以色列的山上。
chapters.en: And I will turn thee back, and leave but the sixth part of thee, and will cause thee to come up from the north parts, and will bring thee upon the mountains of Israel:

chapters.zh: 我必从你左手打落你的弓，从你右手打掉你的箭。
chapters.en: And I will smite thy bow out of thy left hand, and will cause thine arrows to fall out of thy right hand.

chapters.zh: 你和你的军队，并同著你的列国人，都必倒在以色列的山上。我必将你给各类的鸷鸟和田野的走兽作食物。
chapters.en: Thou shalt fall upon the mountains of Israel, thou, and all thy bands, and the people that with thee: I will give thee unto the ravenous birds of every sort, and the beasts of the field to be devoured.

chapters.zh: 你必倒在田野，因为我曾说过。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Thou shalt fall upon the open field: for I have spoken , saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 我要降火在玛各和海岛安然居住的人身上，他们就知道我是耶和华。
chapters.en: And I will send a fire on Magog, and among them that dwell carelessly in the isles: and they shall know that I the LORD.

chapters.zh: 我要在我民以色列中显出我的圣名，也不容我的圣名再被亵渎，列国人就知道我是耶和华 ─ 以色列中的圣者。
chapters.en: So will I make my holy name known in the midst of my people Israel; and I will not pollute my holy name any more: and the heathen shall know that I the LORD, the Holy One in Israel.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华说：这日事情临近，也必成就，乃是我所说的日子。
chapters.en: Behold, it is come, and it is done, saith the Lord GOD; this the day whereof I have spoken.

chapters.zh: 住以色列城邑的人必出去捡器械，就是大小盾牌、弓箭、梃杖、枪矛，都当柴烧火，直烧七年，
chapters.en: And they that dwell in the cities of Israel shall go forth, and shall set on fire and burn the weapons, both the shields and the bucklers, the bows and the arrows, and the handstaves, and the spears, and they shall burn them with fire seven years:

chapters.zh: 甚至他们不必从田野捡柴，也不必从树林伐木；因为他们要用器械烧火，并且抢夺那抢夺他们的人，掳掠那掳掠他们的人。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: So that they shall take no wood out of the field, neither cut down out of the forests; for they shall burn the weapons with fire: and they shall spoil those that spoiled them, and rob those that robbed them, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 当那日，我必将以色列地的谷，就是海东人所经过的谷，赐给歌革为坟地，使经过的人到此停步。在那里人必葬埋歌革和他的群众，就称那地为哈们歌革谷。
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass in that day, I will give unto Gog a place there of graves in Israel, the valley of the passengers on the east of the sea: and it shall stop the of the passengers: and there shall they bury Gog and all his multitude: and they shall call The valley of Hamongog.

chapters.zh: 以色列家的人必用七个月葬埋他们，为要洁净全地。
chapters.en: And seven months shall the house of Israel be burying of them, that they may cleanse the land.

chapters.zh: 全地的居民都必葬埋他们。当我得荣耀的日子，这事必叫他们得名声。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Yea, all the people of the land shall bury ; and it shall be to them a renown the day that I shall be glorified, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 他们必分派人时常巡查遍地，与过路的人一同葬埋那剩在地面上的尸首，好洁净全地。过了七个月，他们还要巡查。
chapters.en: And they shall sever out men of continual employment, passing through the land to bury with the passengers those that remain upon the face of the earth, to cleanse it: after the end of seven months shall they search.

chapters.zh: 巡查遍地的人要经过全地，见有人的骸骨，就在旁边立一标记，等葬埋的人来将骸骨葬在哈们歌革谷。
chapters.en: And the passengers pass through the land, when seeth a man's bone, then shall he set up a sign by it, till the buriers have buried it in the valley of Hamongog.

chapters.zh: 他们必这样洁净那地，并有一城名叫哈摩那。
chapters.en: And also the name of the city Hamonah. Thus shall they cleanse the land.

chapters.zh: 人子啊，主耶和华如此说：你要对各类的飞鸟和田野的走兽说：你们聚集来罢，要从四方聚到我为你们献祭之地，就是在以色列山上献大祭之地，好叫你们吃肉、喝血。
chapters.en: And, thou son of man, thus saith the Lord GOD; Speak unto every feathered fowl, and to every beast of the field, Assemble yourselves, and come; gather yourselves on every side to my sacrifice that I do sacrifice for you, a great sacrifice upon the mountains of Israel, that ye may eat flesh, and drink blood.

chapters.zh: 你们必吃勇士的肉，喝地上首领的血，就如吃公绵羊、羊羔、公山羊、公牛，都是巴珊的肥畜。
chapters.en: Ye shall eat the flesh of the mighty, and drink the blood of the princes of the earth, of rams, of lambs, and of goats, of bullocks, all of them fatlings of Bashan.

chapters.zh: 你们吃我为你们所献的祭，必吃饱了脂油，喝醉了血。
chapters.en: And ye shall eat fat till ye be full, and drink blood till ye be drunken, of my sacrifice which I have sacrificed for you.

chapters.zh: 你们必在我席上饱吃马匹和坐车的人，并勇士和一切的战士。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Thus ye shall be filled at my table with horses and chariots, with mighty men, and with all men of war, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 我必显我的荣耀在列国中；万民就必看见我所行的审判与我在他们身上所加的手。
chapters.en: And I will set my glory among the heathen, and all the heathen shall see my judgment that I have executed, and my hand that I have laid upon them.

chapters.zh: 这样，从那日以后，以色列家必知道我是耶和华 ─ 他们的神。
chapters.en: So the house of Israel shall know that I the LORD their God from that day and forward.

chapters.zh: 列国人也必知道以色列家被掳掠是因他们的罪孽。他们得罪我，我就掩面不顾，将他们交在敌人手中，他们便都倒在刀下。
chapters.en: And the heathen shall know that the house of Israel went into captivity for their iniquity: because they trespassed against me, therefore hid I my face from them, and gave them into the hand of their enemies: so fell they all by the sword.

chapters.zh: 我是照他们的污秽和罪过待他们，并且我掩面不顾他们。
chapters.en: According to their uncleanness and according to their transgressions have I done unto them, and hid my face from them.

chapters.zh: 主耶和华如此说：我要使雅各被掳的人归回，要怜悯以色列全家，又为我的圣名发热心。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Now will I bring again the captivity of Jacob, and have mercy upon the whole house of Israel, and will be jealous for my holy name;

chapters.zh: 他们在本地安然居住，无人惊吓，是我将他们从万民中领回，从仇敌之地召来。我在许多国的民眼前，在他们身上显为圣的时候，他们要担当自己的羞辱和干犯我的一切罪。
chapters.en: After that they have borne their shame, and all their trespasses whereby they have trespassed against me, when they dwelt safely in their land, and none made afraid.

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: When I have brought them again from the people, and gathered them out of their enemies' lands, and am sanctified in them in the sight of many nations;

chapters.zh: 因我使他们被掳到外邦人中，后又聚集他们归回本地，他们就知道我是耶和华 ─ 他们的神；我必不再留他们一人在外邦。
chapters.en: Then shall they know that I the LORD their God, which caused them to be led into captivity among the heathen: but I have gathered them unto their own land, and have left none of them any more there.

chapters.zh: 我也不再掩面不顾他们，因我已将我的灵浇灌以色列家。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Neither will I hide my face any more from them: for I have poured out my spirit upon the house of Israel, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 我们被掳掠第二十五年，耶路撒冷城攻破后十四年，正在年初，月之初十日，耶和华的灵（原文是手）降在我身上，他把我带到以色列地。
chapters.en: In the five and twentieth year of our captivity, in the beginning of the year, in the tenth of the month, in the fourteenth year after that the city was smitten, in the selfsame day the hand of the LORD was upon me, and brought me thither.

chapters.zh: 在神的异象中带我到以色列地，安置在至高的山上；在山上的南边有彷佛一座城建立。
chapters.en: In the visions of God brought he me into the land of Israel, and set me upon a very high mountain, by which as the frame of a city on the south.

chapters.zh: 他带我到那里，见有一人，颜色（原文是形状）如铜，手拿麻绳和量度的竿，站在门口。
chapters.en: And he brought me thither, and, behold, a man, whose appearance like the appearance of brass, with a line of flax in his hand, and a measuring reed; and he stood in the gate.

chapters.zh: 那人对我说：人子啊，凡我所指示你的，你都要用眼看，用耳听，并要放在心上。我带你到这里来，特为要指示你；凡你所见的，你都要告诉以色列家。
chapters.en: And the man said unto me, Son of man, behold with thine eyes, and hear with thine ears, and set thine heart upon all that I shall shew thee; for to the intent that I might shew unto thee thou brought hither: declare all that thou seest to the house of Israel.

chapters.zh: 我见殿四围有墙。那人手拿量度的竿，长六肘，每肘是一肘零一掌。他用竿量墙，厚一竿，高一竿。
chapters.en: And behold a wall on the outside of the house round about, and in the man's hand a measuring reed of six cubits by the cubit and an hand breadth: so he measured the breadth of the building, one reed; and the height, one reed.

chapters.zh: 他到了朝东的门，就上门的台阶，量门的这槛，宽一竿；又量门的那槛，宽一竿。
chapters.en: Then came he unto the gate which looketh toward the east, and went up the stairs thereof, and measured the threshold of the gate, one reed broad; and the other threshold one reed broad.

chapters.zh: 又有卫房，每房长一竿，宽一竿，相隔五肘。门槛，就是挨著向殿的廊门槛，宽一竿。
chapters.en: And little chamber one reed long, and one reed broad; and between the little chambers five cubits; and the threshold of the gate by the porch of the gate within one reed.

chapters.zh: 他又量向殿门的廊子，宽一竿。
chapters.en: He measured also the porch of the gate within, one reed.

chapters.zh: 又量门廊，宽八肘，墙柱厚二肘；那门的廊子向著殿。
chapters.en: Then measured he the porch of the gate, eight cubits; and the posts thereof, two cubits; and the porch of the gate inward.

chapters.zh: 东门洞有卫房：这旁三间，那旁三间，都是一样的尺寸；这边的柱子和那边的柱子，也是一样的尺寸。
chapters.en: And the little chambers of the gate eastward three on this side, and three on that side; they three of one measure: and the posts had one measure on this side and on that side.

chapters.zh: 他量门口，宽十肘，长十三肘。
chapters.en: And he measured the breadth of the entry of the gate, ten cubits; the length of the gate, thirteen cubits.

chapters.zh: 卫房前展出的境界：这边一肘，那边一肘；卫房这边六肘，那边六肘。
chapters.en: The space also before the little chambers one cubit , and the space one cubit on that side: and the little chambers six cubits on this side, and six cubits on that side.

chapters.zh: 又量门洞，从这卫房顶的后檐到那卫房顶的后檐，宽二十五肘；卫房门与门相对。
chapters.en: He measured then the gate from the roof of little chamber to the roof of another: the breadth five and twenty cubits, door against door.

chapters.zh: 又量（原文是造）廊子六十肘（七十士译本是二十肘），墙柱外是院子，有廊为界，在门洞两边。
chapters.en: He made also posts of threescore cubits, even unto the post of the court round about the gate.

chapters.zh: 从大门口到内廊前，共五十肘。
chapters.en: And from the face of the gate of the entrance unto the face of the porch of the inner gate fifty cubits.

chapters.zh: 卫房和门洞两旁柱间并廊子，都有严紧的窗櫺；里边都有窗櫺，柱上有雕刻的棕树。
chapters.en: And narrow windows to the little chambers, and to their posts within the gate round about, and likewise to the arches: and windows round about inward: and upon post palm trees.

chapters.zh: 他带我到外院，见院的四围有铺石地；铺石地上有屋子三十间。
chapters.en: Then brought he me into the outward court, and, lo, chambers, and a pavement made for the court round about: thirty chambers upon the pavement.

chapters.zh: 铺石地，就是矮铺石地在各门洞两旁，以门洞的长短为度。
chapters.en: And the pavement by the side of the gates over against the length of the gates the lower pavement.

chapters.zh: 他从下门量到内院外，共宽一百肘，东面北面都是如此。
chapters.en: Then he measured the breadth from the forefront of the lower gate unto the forefront of the inner court without, an hundred cubits eastward and northward.

chapters.zh: 他量外院朝北的门，长宽若干。
chapters.en: And the gate of the outward court that looked toward the north, he measured the length thereof, and the breadth thereof.

chapters.zh: 门洞的卫房，这旁三间，那旁三间。门洞的柱子和廊子，与第一门的尺寸一样。门洞长五十肘，宽二十五肘。
chapters.en: And the little chambers thereof three on this side and three on that side; and the posts thereof and the arches thereof were after the measure of the first gate: the length thereof fifty cubits, and the breadth five and twenty cubits.

chapters.zh: 其窗櫺和廊子，并雕刻的棕树，与朝东的门尺寸一样。登七层台阶上到这门，前面有廊子。
chapters.en: And their windows, and their arches, and their palm trees, after the measure of the gate that looketh toward the east; and they went up unto it by seven steps; and the arches thereof before them.

chapters.zh: 内院有门与这门相对，北面东面都是如此。他从这门量到那门，共一百肘。
chapters.en: And the gate of the inner court over against the gate toward the north, and toward the east; and he measured from gate to gate an hundred cubits.

chapters.zh: 他带我往南去，见朝南有门，又照先前的尺寸量门洞的柱子和廊子。
chapters.en: After that he brought me toward the south, and behold a gate toward the south: and he measured the posts thereof and the arches thereof according to these measures.

chapters.zh: 门洞两旁与廊子的周围都有窗櫺，和先量的窗櫺一样。门洞长五十肘，宽二十五肘。
chapters.en: And windows in it and in the arches thereof round about, like those windows: the length fifty cubits, and the breadth five and twenty cubits.

chapters.zh: 登七层台阶上到这门，前面有廊子；柱上有雕刻的棕树，这边一棵，那边一棵。
chapters.en: And seven steps to go up to it, and the arches thereof before them: and it had palm trees, one on this side, and another on that side, upon the posts thereof.

chapters.zh: 内院朝南有门。从这门量到朝南的那门，共一百肘。
chapters.en: And a gate in the inner court toward the south: and he measured from gate to gate toward the south an hundred cubits.

chapters.zh: 他带我从南门到内院，就照先前的尺寸量南门。
chapters.en: And he brought me to the inner court by the south gate: and he measured the south gate according to these measures;

chapters.zh: 卫房和柱子，并廊子都照先前的尺寸。门洞两旁与廊子的周围都有窗櫺。门洞长五十肘，宽二十五肘。
chapters.en: And the little chambers thereof, and the posts thereof, and the arches thereof, according to these measures: and windows in it and in the arches thereof round about: fifty cubits long, and five and twenty cubits broad.

chapters.zh: 周围有廊子，长二十五肘，宽五肘。
chapters.en: And the arches round about five and twenty cubits long, and five cubits broad.

chapters.zh: 廊子朝著外院，柱上有雕刻的棕树。登八层台阶上到这门。
chapters.en: And the arches thereof toward the utter court; and palm trees upon the posts thereof: and the going up to it eight steps.

chapters.zh: 他带我到内院的东面，就照先前的尺寸量东门。
chapters.en: And he brought me into the inner court toward the east: and he measured the gate according to these measures.

chapters.zh: 卫房和柱子，并廊子都照先前的尺寸。门洞两旁与廊子的周围都有窗櫺。门洞长五十肘，宽二十五肘。
chapters.en: And the little chambers thereof, and the posts thereof, and the arches thereof, according to these measures: and windows therein and in the arches thereof round about: fifty cubits long, and five and twenty cubits broad.

chapters.zh: 廊子朝著外院。门洞两旁的柱子都有雕刻的棕树。登八层台阶上到这门。
chapters.en: And the arches thereof toward the outward court; and palm trees upon the posts thereof, on this side, and on that side: and the going up to it eight steps.

chapters.zh: 他带我到北门，就照先前的尺寸量那门，
chapters.en: And he brought me to the north gate, and measured according to these measures;

chapters.zh: 就是量卫房和柱子，并廊子。门洞周围都有窗櫺；门洞长五十肘，宽二十五肘。
chapters.en: The little chambers thereof, the posts thereof, and the arches thereof, and the windows to it round about: the length fifty cubits, and the breadth five and twenty cubits.

chapters.zh: 廊柱朝著外院。门洞两旁的柱子都有雕刻的棕树。登八层台阶上到这门。
chapters.en: And the posts thereof toward the utter court; and palm trees upon the posts thereof, on this side, and on that side: and the going up to it eight steps.

chapters.zh: 门洞的柱旁有屋子和门；祭司（原文是他们）在那里洗燔祭牲。
chapters.en: And the chambers and the entries thereof by the posts of the gates, where they washed the burnt offering.

chapters.zh: 在门廊内，这边有两张桌子，那边有两张桌子，在其上可以宰杀燔祭牲、赎罪祭牲，和赎愆祭牲。
chapters.en: And in the porch of the gate two tables on this side, and two tables on that side, to slay thereon the burnt offering and the sin offering and the trespass offering.

chapters.zh: 上到朝北的门口，这边有两张桌子，门廊那边也有两张桌子。
chapters.en: And at the side without, as one goeth up to the entry of the north gate, two tables; and on the other side, which at the porch of the gate, two tables.

chapters.zh: 门这边有四张桌子，那边有四张桌子，共八张；在其上祭司宰杀牺牲。
chapters.en: Four tables on this side, and four tables on that side, by the side of the gate; eight tables, whereupon they slew .

chapters.zh: 为燔祭牲有四张桌子，是凿过的石头做成的，长一肘半，宽一肘半，高一肘。祭司将宰杀燔祭牲和平安祭牲所用的器皿放在其上。
chapters.en: And the four tables of hewn stone for the burnt offering, of a cubit and an half long, and a cubit and an half broad, and one cubit high: whereupon also they laid the instruments wherewith they slew the burnt offering and the sacrifice.

chapters.zh: 有钩子，宽一掌，钉在廊内的四围。桌子上有牺牲的肉。
chapters.en: And within hooks, an hand broad, fastened round about: and upon the tables the flesh of the offering.

chapters.zh: 在北门旁，内院里有屋子，为歌唱的人而设。这屋子朝南（南：原文是东）；在南门旁，又有一间朝北。
chapters.en: And without the inner gate the chambers of the singers in the inner court, which at the side of the north gate; and their prospect toward the south: one at the side of the east gate the prospect toward the north.

chapters.zh: 他对我说：这朝南的屋子是为看守殿宇的祭司；
chapters.en: And he said unto me, This chamber, whose prospect toward the south, for the priests, the keepers of the charge of the house.

chapters.zh: 那朝北的屋子是为看守祭坛的祭司。这些祭司是利未人中撒督的子孙，近前来事奉耶和华的。
chapters.en: And the chamber whose prospect toward the north for the priests, the keepers of the charge of the altar: these the sons of Zadok among the sons of Levi, which come near to the LORD to minister unto him.

chapters.zh: 他又量内院，长一百肘，宽一百肘，是见方的。祭坛在殿前。
chapters.en: So he measured the court, an hundred cubits long, and an hundred cubits broad, foursquare; and the altar before the house.

chapters.zh: 于是他带我到殿前的廊子，量廊子的墙柱。这面厚五肘，那面厚五肘。门两旁，这边三肘，那边三肘。
chapters.en: And he brought me to the porch of the house, and measured post of the porch, five cubits on this side, and five cubits on that side: and the breadth of the gate three cubits on this side, and three cubits on that side.

chapters.zh: 廊子长二十肘，宽十一肘。上廊子有台阶。靠近墙柱又有柱子，这边一根，那边一根。
chapters.en: The length of the porch twenty cubits, and the breadth eleven cubits; and by the steps whereby they went up to it: and pillars by the posts, one on this side, and another on that side.

chapters.zh: 他带我到殿那里量墙柱：这面厚六肘，那面厚六肘，宽窄与会幕相同。
chapters.en: Afterward he brought me to the temple, and measured the posts, six cubits broad on the one side, and six cubits broad on the other side, the breadth of the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 门口宽十肘。门两旁，这边五肘，那边五肘。他量殿长四十肘，宽二十肘。
chapters.en: And the breadth of the door ten cubits; and the sides of the door five cubits on the one side, and five cubits on the other side: and he measured the length thereof, forty cubits: and the breadth, twenty cubits.

chapters.zh: 他到内殿量墙柱，各厚二肘。门口宽六肘，门两旁各宽七肘。
chapters.en: Then went he inward, and measured the post of the door, two cubits; and the door, six cubits; and the breadth of the door, seven cubits.

chapters.zh: 他量内殿，长二十肘，宽二十肘。他对我说：这是至圣所。
chapters.en: So he measured the length thereof, twenty cubits; and the breadth, twenty cubits, before the temple: and he said unto me, This the most holy .

chapters.zh: 他又量殿墙，厚六肘；围著殿有旁屋，各宽四肘。
chapters.en: After he measured the wall of the house, six cubits; and the breadth of side chamber, four cubits, round about the house on every side.

chapters.zh: 旁屋有三层，层叠而上，每层排列三十间。旁屋的梁木搁在殿墙坎上，免得插入殿墙。
chapters.en: And the side chambers three, one over another, and thirty in order; and they entered into the wall which of the house for the side chambers round about, that they might have hold, but they had not hold in the wall of the house.

chapters.zh: 这围殿的旁屋越高越宽；因旁屋围殿悬叠而上，所以越上越宽，从下一层，由中一层，到上一层。
chapters.en: And an enlarging, and a winding about still upward to the side chambers: for the winding about of the house went still upward round about the house: therefore the breadth of the house upward, and so increased the lowest to the highest by the midst.

chapters.zh: 我又见围著殿有高月台。旁屋的根基，高足一竿，就是六大肘。
chapters.en: I saw also the height of the house round about: the foundations of the side chambers a full reed of six great cubits.

chapters.zh: 旁屋的外墙厚五肘。旁屋之外还有余地。
chapters.en: The thickness of the wall, which for the side chamber without, five cubits: and which left the place of the side chambers that within.

chapters.zh: 在旁屋与对面的房屋中间有空地，宽二十肘。
chapters.en: And between the chambers the wideness of twenty cubits round about the house on every side.

chapters.zh: 旁屋的门都向余地：一门向北，一门向南。周围的余地宽五肘。
chapters.en: And the doors of the side chambers toward left, one door toward the north, and another door toward the south: and the breadth of the place that was left five cubits round about.

chapters.zh: 在西面空地之后有房子，宽七十肘，长九十肘，墙四围厚五肘。
chapters.en: Now the building that before the separate place at the end toward the west seventy cubits broad; and the wall of the building five cubits thick round about, and the length thereof ninety cubits.

chapters.zh: 这样，他量殿，长一百肘，又量空地和那房子并墙，共长一百肘。
chapters.en: So he measured the house, an hundred cubits long; and the separate place, and the building, with the walls thereof, an hundred cubits long;

chapters.zh: 殿的前面和两旁的空地，宽一百肘。
chapters.en: Also the breadth of the face of the house, and of the separate place toward the east, an hundred cubits.

chapters.zh: 他量空地后面的那房子，并两旁的楼廊，共长一百肘。
chapters.en: And he measured the length of the building over against the separate place which behind it, and the galleries thereof on the one side and on the other side, an hundred cubits, with the inner temple, and the porches of the court;

chapters.zh: 内殿、院廊、门槛、严紧的窗櫺，并对著门槛的三层楼廊，从地到窗櫺（窗櫺都有蔽子），
chapters.en: The door posts, and the narrow windows, and the galleries round about on their three stories, over against the door, cieled with wood round about, and from the ground up to the windows, and the windows covered;

chapters.zh: 直到门以上，就是到内殿和外殿内外四围墙壁，都按尺寸用木板遮蔽。
chapters.en: To that above the door, even unto the inner house, and without, and by all the wall round about within and without, by measure.

chapters.zh: 墙上雕刻基路伯和棕树。每二基路伯中间有一棵棕树，每基路伯有二脸。
chapters.en: And made with cherubims and palm trees, so that a palm tree between a cherub and a cherub; and cherub had two faces;

chapters.zh: 这边有人脸向著棕树，那边有狮子脸向著棕树，殿内周围都是如此。
chapters.en: So that the face of a man toward the palm tree on the one side, and the face of a young lion toward the palm tree on the other side: made through all the house round about.

chapters.zh: 从地至门以上，都有基路伯和棕树。殿墙就是这样。
chapters.en: From the ground unto above the door cherubims and palm trees made, and the wall of the temple.

chapters.zh: 殿的门柱是方的。至圣所的前面，形状和殿的形状一样。
chapters.en: The posts of the temple squared, the face of the sanctuary; the appearance as the appearance .

chapters.zh: 坛是木头做的，高三肘，长二肘。坛角和坛面，并四旁，都是木头做的。他对我说：这是耶和华面前的桌子。
